{{draft categories| [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines]] [[Category:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (disambiguation)]] [[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:short pages that need to be lengthened so that this properly works]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[en:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines]] [[sn:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (disambiguation)]]
[[category:completion tier 1]][[ja:鷲宮神社]][[de:Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein]]

[[File:酉の市Torinoichi000.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Torinoichi|de|Torinoichi|ja|酉の市|zh|酉の市}}の{{ill|Kumade|de|Kumade|ja|熊手|zh|熊手}}]] '''Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''' (鷲宮神社) is the name of a {{ill|shrine|de|Schrein|ja|神社|zh|神社}}.
It is a branch of the Ōtori faith based on {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|de|Ōtori-Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|zh|大鳥大社}}, though in many cases a Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine serves as the head shrine of the Kantō region’s {{ill|O‑Tori‑sama|de|O‑Tori‑sama|ja|お酉様|zh|O‑Tori‑sama}} cult – notably the one enshrined in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Präfektur Saitama|ja|埼玉県|zh|埼玉县}}’s {{ill|Kuki City|de|Kuki Stadt|ja|久喜市|zh|久喜市}}. The Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine in Kuki City was the head shrine (総社) of the {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|de|Fujiwara no Hidesato Ota-Clan|ja|太田氏|zh|太田氏|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato's|lt_ja=Fujiwara no Hidesato流太田氏|lt_de=Fujiwara no Hidesato Ota|lt_zh=Fujiwara no Hidesato流太田氏}} {{ill|Ota Villa|de|Ota-Anwesen|ja|太田荘|zh|太田荘}}, and many branch shrines exist mainly along the {{ill|Nakagawa|de|Nakagawa|ja|中川|zh|中川}} region. In other areas, however, Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines are merely branches of other Ōtori faith shrines and may have no connection to the Kuki City shrine.
In addition, along the western part of the {{ill|Arakawa River|de|Arakawa|ja|荒川 (関東)|zh|Arakawa River|lt=Arakawa|lt_ja=荒川|lt_de=Arakawa|lt_zh=Arakawa}} basin there is the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|de|Hikawa-Schrein|ja|氷川神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|氷川神社}}, along the {{ill|Moto-Arakawa|de|Moto-Arakawa|ja|元荒川|zh|元荒川}} basin there is the {{ill|Kuizu Shrine|de|Kuizu-Schrein|ja|久伊豆神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|久伊豆神社}}, and in the eastern and northern parts along the {{ill|Tone River|de|Tone Fluss|ja|利根川|zh|利根川}} and {{ill|Edogawa River|de|Edogawa-Fluss|ja|江戸川|zh|江戸川}} basins, many {{ill|Katori Shrine|de|Katori-Schrein|ja|香取神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|香取神社}} are found. Occasionally, merged shrines such as {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|鷲香取神社}} or {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|香取鷲神社}} appear. Furthermore, since the term “宮” means “shrine,” many places today label their shrines as {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|鷲神社}} or {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|日鷲神社}}, even when their old or common names remain Washi‑no‑Miya. For example, there is the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|鷲神社 (台東区)}}, in Taitō Ward, Tokyo (as inscribed on its torii plaque), the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|鷲神社 (土浦市)}}, in Tsuchiura City, Ibaraki, and the {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma City)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|日鷲神社}}, in Minamisoma City, Fukushima.
As a {{ill|religious corporation|de|Religiöse Körperschaft|ja|宗教法人|zh|宗教法人}}, there are nationwide 22 Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines and 2 Washi‑no‑Miya Associations.
See also {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|de|Ōtori-Schrein|ja|大鳥神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鳥神社}}, {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|鷲神社}}, {{ill|Great Washi Shrine|de|Großer Washi-Schrein|ja|大鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鷲神社}}, and {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|日鷲神社}}.

[[category:completion tier 1]][[ja:鷲宮神社]][[de:Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein]]

[[File:酉の市Torinoichi000.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Torinoichi|de|Torinoichi|ja|酉の市|zh|酉の市}}の{{ill|Kumade|de|Kumade|ja|熊手|zh|熊手}}]] '''Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''' (鷲宮神社) is the name of a {{ill|shrine|de|Schrein|ja|神社|zh|神社}}.
It is a branch of the Ōtori faith based on {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|de|Ōtori-Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|zh|大鳥大社}}, though in many cases a Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine serves as the head shrine of the Kantō region’s {{ill|O‑Tori‑sama|de|O‑Tori‑sama|ja|お酉様|zh|O‑Tori‑sama}} cult – notably the one enshrined in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Präfektur Saitama|ja|埼玉県|zh|埼玉县}}’s {{ill|Kuki City|de|Kuki Stadt|ja|久喜市|zh|久喜市}}. The Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine in Kuki City was the head shrine (総社) of the {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|de|Fujiwara no Hidesato Ota-Clan|ja|太田氏|zh|太田氏|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato's|lt_ja=Fujiwara no Hidesato流太田氏|lt_de=Fujiwara no Hidesato Ota|lt_zh=Fujiwara no Hidesato流太田氏}} {{ill|Ota Villa|de|Ota-Anwesen|ja|太田荘|zh|太田荘}}, and many branch shrines exist mainly along the {{ill|Nakagawa|de|Nakagawa|ja|中川|zh|中川}} region. In other areas, however, Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines are merely branches of other Ōtori faith shrines and may have no connection to the Kuki City shrine.
In addition, along the western part of the {{ill|Arakawa River|de|Arakawa|ja|荒川 (関東)|zh|Arakawa River|lt=Arakawa|lt_ja=荒川|lt_de=Arakawa|lt_zh=Arakawa}} basin there is the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|de|Hikawa-Schrein|ja|氷川神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|氷川神社}}, along the {{ill|Moto-Arakawa|de|Moto-Arakawa|ja|元荒川|zh|元荒川}} basin there is the {{ill|Kuizu Shrine|de|Kuizu-Schrein|ja|久伊豆神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|久伊豆神社}}, and in the eastern and northern parts along the {{ill|Tone River|de|Tone Fluss|ja|利根川|zh|利根川}} and {{ill|Edogawa River|de|Edogawa-Fluss|ja|江戸川|zh|江戸川}} basins, many {{ill|Katori Shrine|de|Katori-Schrein|ja|香取神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|香取神社}} are found. Occasionally, merged shrines such as {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|鷲香取神社}} or {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|香取鷲神社}} appear. Furthermore, since the term “宮” means “shrine,” many places today label their shrines as {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|鷲神社}} or {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|日鷲神社}}, even when their old or common names remain Washi‑no‑Miya. For example, there is the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|鷲神社 (台東区)}}, in Taitō Ward, Tokyo (as inscribed on its torii plaque), the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|鷲神社 (土浦市)}}, in Tsuchiura City, Ibaraki, and the {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma City)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|日鷲神社}}, in Minamisoma City, Fukushima.
As a {{ill|religious corporation|de|Religiöse Körperschaft|ja|宗教法人|zh|宗教法人}}, there are nationwide 22 Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines and 2 Washi‑no‑Miya Associations.
See also {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|de|Ōtori-Schrein|ja|大鳥神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鳥神社}}, {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|鷲神社}}, {{ill|Great Washi Shrine|de|Großer Washi-Schrein|ja|大鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鷲神社}}, and {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|日鷲神社}}.
== Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines ==
=== Fukushima Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Koriyama)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Koriyama)|ja|鷲宮神社 (郡山市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Koriyama)}} – Enshrined at Suginoiri, Yatagawa, Tamura‑chō, Koriyama City.
=== Ibaraki Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kawachi Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Kawachi-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (河内町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kawachi Town)}} – Enshrined at Kofabayashi, Kawachi‑chō, Inashiki District. The furigana for the corporation name is “ワシミヤジンジャ.”
=== Tochigi Prefecture === * '''{{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Toga Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Tōchigi, Toga-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (栃木市都賀町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Toga Town)}}''' ('''Shimotsukuni Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''') – Enshrined at Ienaka, Toga Town, Tōchigi City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Hakamori Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Tōchigi, Hakamori-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (栃木市箱森町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Hakamori Town)}} – Enshrined at Hakamori Town, Tōchigi City. <!--** {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Utsunomiya)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Utsunomiya)|ja|鷲宮神社 (宇都宮市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Utsunomiya)}} – Enshrined at Sarinotani, Utsunomiya City. Not a religious corporation, hence temporarily commented out --> * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Sakura)|ja|鷲宮神社 (さくら市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City)}} – Enshrined at Washiyado, Sakura City. <!--* {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City, Ujiie)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Sakura, Ujiie)|ja|鷲宮神社 (さくら市氏家)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City, Ujiie)}} – Enshrined at Ujiie, Sakura City. Not a religious corporation, hence temporarily commented out --> * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sano City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Sano)|ja|鷲宮神社 (佐野市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sano City)}} – Enshrined at Inubushi Kamimachi, Sano City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shimotsuke City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Shimotsuke)|ja|鷲宮神社 (下野市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shimotsuke City)}} – Enshrined at Hashimoto, Shimotsuke City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Ishijima)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Moka, Ishijima)|ja|鷲宮神社 (真岡市石島)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Ishijima)}} – Enshrined at Ishijima, Moka City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Washisu)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Moka, Washisu)|ja|鷲宮神社 (真岡市鷲巣)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Washisu)}} – Enshrined at Washisu, Moka City.
=== Chiba Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Chiba City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Chiba)|ja|鷲宮神社 (千葉市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Chiba City)}} – Enshrined at Daiganji-chō, Chūō-ku, Chiba City.
=== Saitama Prefecture === * '''{{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein|ja|鷲宮神社|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine}}''' ('''Musashi‑no‑Kuni Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''') – Enshrined at Washi‑no‑Miya, Kuki City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Association (Kuki City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Gesellschaft (Kuki)|ja|鷲宮社 (久喜市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Association (Kuki City)}} – Enshrined at Hachihō, Kuki City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kuki City, 713 Shimosayami)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Kuki, 713 Shimosayami)|ja|鷲宮神社 (久喜市下早見713)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kuki City, 713 Shimosayami)}} – Enshrined at address 713 Shimosayami, Kuki City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kuki City, 1766 Shimosayami)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Kuki, 1766 Shimosayami)|ja|鷲宮神社 (久喜市下早見1766)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kuki City, 1766 Shimosayami)}} – Enshrined at address 1766 Shimosayami, Kuki City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Saitama City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Saitama)|ja|鷲宮神社 (さいたま市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Saitama City)}} – Enshrined at Ueno, Iwatsuki-ku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kazo City, Hanazaki)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Kazo, Hanazaki)|ja|鷲宮神社 (加須市花崎)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kazo City, Hanazaki)}} – Enshrined at Hanazaki, Kazo City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kazo City, Hisashita)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Kazo, Hisashita)|ja|鷲宮神社 (加須市久下)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kazo City, Hisashita)}} – Enshrined at Hisashita, Kazo City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shiraoka City, Uenoda)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Shiraoka, Uenoda)|ja|鷲宮神社 (白岡市上野田)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shiraoka City, Uenoda)}} – Enshrined at Uenoda, Shiraoka City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shiraoka City, Shimonoda)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Shiraoka, Shimonoda)|ja|鷲宮神社 (白岡市下野田)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shiraoka City, Shimonoda)}} – Enshrined at Shimonoda, Shiraoka City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Hanyu City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Hanyu)|ja|鷲宮神社 (羽生市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Hanyu City)}} – Enshrined at Hideyasu, Hanyu City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Fukaya City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Fukaya)|ja|鷲宮神社 (深谷市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Fukaya City)}} – Enshrined at Takabata, Fukaya City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Miyashiro Town, Higashi‑Kumebara)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Miyashiro, Higashi‑Kumebara)|ja|鷲宮神社 (宮代町東粂原)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Miyashiro Town, Higashi‑Kumebara)}} – Enshrined at Higashi‑Kumebara, Miyashiro Town, Minami‑Saitama District. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Miyashiro Town, Nishi‑Kumebara)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Miyashiro, Nishi‑Kumebara)|ja|鷲宮神社 (宮代町西粂原)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Miyashiro Town, Nishi‑Kumebara)}} – Enshrined at Nishi‑Kumebara, Miyashiro Town, Minami‑Saitama District. The corporation name is “Religious Corporation Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine.”
=== Yamanashi Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Association (Nirasaki City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Gesellschaft (Nirasaki)|ja|鷲宮社 (韮崎市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Association (Nirasaki City)}} – Enshrined at Anayamachō, Nirasaki City.
== Alternate Names, Former Names, and Merged Shrines ==
=== Fukushima Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)}} – Enshrined at Akechi, Onaba, Kodaka District, Minamisoma City.
=== Ibaraki Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|de|Sashinō-Schrein (Ishioka, Someya)|ja|佐志能神社 (石岡市染谷)|zh|Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)}} – Enshrined at Someya, Ishioka City. * {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|Katori Washi Shrine}} – Enshrined at Komagome, Koga City. * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura, Higashizakichō)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)}} – Enshrined at Higashizakichō, Tsuchiura City. * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Naka City)|de|Washi-Schrein (Naka)|ja|鷲神社 (那珂市)|zh|Washi Shrine (Naka City)}} – Enshrined at Washi‑no‑Miya, Hongosu, Naka City.
=== Saitama Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|de|Washi-Schrein (Saitama, Iwatsuki Sueta)|ja|鷲神社 (さいたま市岩槻区末田)|zh|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)}} – Enshrined at Sueta, Iwatsuki-ku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Aoge Gochū Shrine|de|Aoge Gochū-Schrein|ja|青毛五柱神社|zh|Aoge Gochū Shrine}} – Enshrined at Aoge, Kuki City. * {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine}} – Enshrined at Shōbu Town, Kuki City. It enshrines as its principal deities the mother {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|zh|Kushinadahime|lt=Inada‑hime|lt_ja=稲田姫命|lt_de=Inada‑hime|lt_zh=稻田姫命}}, also known as {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine|lt=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_ja=袋田大明神|lt_de=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_zh=Fukuroda Daimyōjin}}, the child {{ill|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|de|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|ja|建比良鳥命|zh|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt=Wuyi Tori no Mikoto|lt_ja=武夷鳥命|lt_de=Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt_zh=武夷鳥命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|de|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|zh|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|lt_de=Ōkuninushi|lt_zh=大国主}} (also known as {{ill|Tamatsukushi Shrine|de|Tamatsukushi-Schrein|ja|玉敷神社|zh|Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt=Kuizu Daimyōjin|lt_ja=久伊豆大明神|lt_de=Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt_zh=Kuizu Daimyōjin}}). * {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|Washi Katori Shrine}} – Enshrined at Uchimaki, Kasukabe City.
=== Tokyo Metropolis === * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|Washi Shrine (Taitō)}} – Enshrined at Senshuku, Taitō Ward.

It is a branch of the Ōtori faith based on {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|de|Ōtori-Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|zh|大鳥大社}}, though in many cases a Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine serves as the head shrine of the Kantō region’s {{ill|O‑Tori‑sama|de|O‑Tori‑sama|ja|お酉様|zh|O‑Tori‑sama}} cult – notably the one enshrined in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Präfektur Saitama|ja|埼玉県|zh|埼玉县}}’s {{ill|Kuki City|de|Kuki Stadt|ja|久喜市|zh|久喜市}}. The Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine in Kuki City was the head shrine (総社) of the {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|de|Fujiwara no Hidesato Ota-Clan|ja|太田氏|zh|太田氏|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato's|lt_ja=Fujiwara no Hidesato流太田氏|lt_de=Fujiwara no Hidesato Ota|lt_zh=Fujiwara no Hidesato流太田氏}} {{ill|Ota Villa|de|Ota-Anwesen|ja|太田荘|zh|太田荘}}, and many branch shrines exist mainly along the {{ill|Nakagawa|de|Nakagawa|ja|中川|zh|中川}} region. In other areas, however, Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines are merely branches of other Ōtori faith shrines and may have no connection to the Kuki City shrine.
In addition, along the western part of the {{ill|Arakawa River|de|Arakawa|ja|荒川 (関東)|zh|Arakawa River|lt=Arakawa|lt_ja=荒川|lt_de=Arakawa|lt_zh=Arakawa}} basin there is the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|de|Hikawa-Schrein|ja|氷川神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|氷川神社}}, along the {{ill|Moto-Arakawa|de|Moto-Arakawa|ja|元荒川|zh|元荒川}} basin there is the {{ill|Kuizu Shrine|de|Kuizu-Schrein|ja|久伊豆神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|久伊豆神社}}, and in the eastern and northern parts along the {{ill|Tone River|de|Tone Fluss|ja|利根川|zh|利根川}} and {{ill|Edogawa River|de|Edogawa-Fluss|ja|江戸川|zh|江戸川}} basins, many {{ill|Katori Shrine|de|Katori-Schrein|ja|香取神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|香取神社}} are found. Occasionally, merged shrines such as {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|鷲香取神社}} or {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|香取鷲神社}} appear. For example, there is the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|鷲神社 (台東区)}}, in Taitō Ward, Tokyo (as inscribed on its torii plaque), the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|鷲神社 (土浦市)}}, in Tsuchiura City, Ibaraki, and the {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma City)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|日鷲神社}}, in Minamisoma City, Fukushima.
As a {{ill|religious corporation|de|Religiöse Körperschaft|ja|宗教法人|zh|宗教法人}}, there are nationwide 22 Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines and 2 Washi‑no‑Miya Associations.
See also {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|de|Ōtori-Schrein|ja|大鳥神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鳥神社}}, {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|鷲神社}}, {{ill|Great Washi Shrine|de|Großer Washi-Schrein|ja|大鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鷲神社}}, and {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|日鷲神社}}.
== Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines ==
=== Fukushima Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Koriyama)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Koriyama)|ja|鷲宮神社 (郡山市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Koriyama)}} – Enshrined at Suginoiri, Yatagawa, Tamura‑chō, Koriyama City.
=== Ibaraki Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kawachi Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Kawachi-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (河内町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kawachi Town)}} – Enshrined at Kofabayashi, Kawachi‑chō, Inashiki District. The furigana for the corporation name is “ワシミヤジンジャ.”
=== Tochigi Prefecture === * '''{{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Toga Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Tōchigi, Toga-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (栃木市都賀町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Toga Town)}}''' ('''Shimotsukuni Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''') – Enshrined at Ienaka, Toga Town, Tōchigi City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Hakamori Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Tōchigi, Hakamori-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (栃木市箱森町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Hakamori Town)}} – Enshrined at Hakamori Town, Tōchigi City. <!--** {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Utsunomiya)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Utsunomiya)|ja|鷲宮神社 (宇都宮市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Utsunomiya)}} – Enshrined at Sarinotani, Utsunomiya City. Not a religious corporation, hence temporarily commented out --> * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Sakura)|ja|鷲宮神社 (さくら市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City)}} – Enshrined at Washiyado, Sakura City. <!--* {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City, Ujiie)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Sakura, Ujiie)|ja|鷲宮神社 (さくら市氏家)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City, Ujiie)}} – Enshrined at Ujiie, Sakura City. Not a religious corporation, hence temporarily commented out --> * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sano City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Sano)|ja|鷲宮神社 (佐野市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sano City)}} – Enshrined at Inubushi Kamimachi, Sano City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shimotsuke City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Shimotsuke)|ja|鷲宮神社 (下野市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shimotsuke City)}} – Enshrined at Hashimoto, Shimotsuke City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Ishijima)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Moka, Ishijima)|ja|鷲宮神社 (真岡市石島)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Ishijima)}} – Enshrined at Ishijima, Moka City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Washisu)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Moka, Washisu)|ja|鷲宮神社 (真岡市鷲巣)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Washisu)}} – Enshrined at Washisu, Moka City.

=== Ibaraki Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|de|Sashinō-Schrein (Ishioka, Someya)|ja|佐志能神社 (石岡市染谷)|zh|Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)}} – Enshrined at Someya, Ishioka City. * {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|Katori Washi Shrine}} – Enshrined at Komagome, Koga City. * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura, Higashizakichō)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)}} – Enshrined at Higashizakichō, Tsuchiura City. * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Naka City)|de|Washi-Schrein (Naka)|ja|鷲神社 (那珂市)|zh|Washi Shrine (Naka City)}} – Enshrined at Washi‑no‑Miya, Hongosu, Naka City.
=== Saitama Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|de|Washi-Schrein (Saitama, Iwatsuki Sueta)|ja|鷲神社 (さいたま市岩槻区末田)|zh|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)}} – Enshrined at Sueta, Iwatsuki-ku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Aoge Gochū Shrine|de|Aoge Gochū-Schrein|ja|青毛五柱神社|zh|Aoge Gochū Shrine}} – Enshrined at Aoge, Kuki City. * {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine}} – Enshrined at Shōbu Town, Kuki City. It enshrines as its principal deities the mother {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|zh|Kushinadahime|lt=Inada‑hime|lt_ja=稲田姫命|lt_de=Inada‑hime|lt_zh=稻田姫命}}, also known as {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine|lt=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_ja=袋田大明神|lt_de=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_zh=Fukuroda Daimyōjin}}, the child {{ill|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|de|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|ja|建比良鳥命|zh|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt=Wuyi Tori no Mikoto|lt_ja=武夷鳥命|lt_de=Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt_zh=武夷鳥命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|de|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|zh|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|lt_de=Ōkuninushi|lt_zh=大国主}} (also known as {{ill|Tamatsukushi Shrine|de|Tamatsukushi-Schrein|ja|玉敷神社|zh|Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt=Kuizu Daimyōjin|lt_ja=久伊豆大明神|lt_de=Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt_zh=Kuizu Daimyōjin}}). * {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|Washi Katori Shrine}} – Enshrined at Uchimaki, Kasukabe City.
=== Tokyo Metropolis === * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|Washi Shrine (Taitō)}} – Enshrined at Senshuku, Taitō Ward.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:酉の市Torinoichi000.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Torinoichi|de|Torinoichi|ja|酉の市|zh|酉の市}}の{{ill|Kumade|de|Kumade|ja|熊手|zh|熊手}}]] '''Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''' (鷲宮神社) is the name of a {{ill|shrine|de|Schrein|ja|神社|zh|神社}}.
It is a branch of the Ōtori faith based on {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|de|Ōtori-Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|zh|大鳥大社}}, though in many cases a Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine serves as the head shrine of the Kantō region’s {{ill|O‑Tori‑sama|de|O‑Tori‑sama|ja|お酉様|zh|O‑Tori‑sama}} cult – notably the one enshrined in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Präfektur Saitama|ja|埼玉県|zh|埼玉县}}’s {{ill|Kuki City|de|Kuki Stadt|ja|久喜市|zh|久喜市}}. The Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine in Kuki City was the head shrine (総社) of the {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|de|Fujiwara no Hidesato Ota-Clan|ja|太田氏|zh|太田氏|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato's|lt_ja=Fujiwara no Hidesato流太田氏|lt_de=Fujiwara no Hidesato Ota|lt_zh=Fujiwara no Hidesato流太田氏}} {{ill|Ota Villa|de|Ota-Anwesen|ja|太田荘|zh|太田荘}}, and many branch shrines exist mainly along the {{ill|Nakagawa|de|Nakagawa|ja|中川|zh|中川}} region. In other areas, however, Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines are merely branches of other Ōtori faith shrines and may have no connection to the Kuki City shrine.
In addition, along the western part of the {{ill|Arakawa River|de|Arakawa|ja|荒川 (関東)|zh|Arakawa River|lt=Arakawa|lt_ja=荒川|lt_de=Arakawa|lt_zh=Arakawa}} basin there is the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|de|Hikawa-Schrein|ja|氷川神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|氷川神社}}, along the {{ill|Moto-Arakawa|de|Moto-Arakawa|ja|元荒川|zh|元荒川}} basin there is the {{ill|Kuizu Shrine|de|Kuizu-Schrein|ja|久伊豆神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|久伊豆神社}}, and in the eastern and northern parts along the {{ill|Tone River|de|Tone Fluss|ja|利根川|zh|利根川}} and {{ill|Edogawa River|de|Edogawa-Fluss|ja|江戸川|zh|江戸川}} basins, many {{ill|Katori Shrine|de|Katori-Schrein|ja|香取神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|香取神社}} are found. Occasionally, merged shrines such as {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|鷲香取神社}} or {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|香取鷲神社}} appear. Furthermore, since the term “宮” means “shrine,” many places today label their shrines as {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|鷲神社}} or {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|日鷲神社}}, even when their old or common names remain Washi‑no‑Miya. For example, there is the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|鷲神社 (台東区)}}, in Taitō Ward, Tokyo (as inscribed on its torii plaque), the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|鷲神社 (土浦市)}}, in Tsuchiura City, Ibaraki, and the {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma City)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|日鷲神社}}, in Minamisoma City, Fukushima.
As a {{ill|religious corporation|de|Religiöse Körperschaft|ja|宗教法人|zh|宗教法人}}, there are nationwide 22 Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines and 2 Washi‑no‑Miya Associations.
See also {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|de|Ōtori-Schrein|ja|大鳥神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鳥神社}}, {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|鷲神社}}, {{ill|Great Washi Shrine|de|Großer Washi-Schrein|ja|大鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鷲神社}}, and {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|日鷲神社}}.

=== Chiba Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Chiba City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Chiba)|ja|鷲宮神社 (千葉市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Chiba City)}} – Enshrined at Daiganji-chō, Chūō-ku, Chiba City.

{{draft categories| [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines]] [[Category:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (disambiguation)]] [[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:short pages that need to be lengthened so that this properly works]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] }} [[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[en:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines]] [[sn:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (disambiguation)]]
[[category:completion tier 1]][[ja:鷲宮神社]][[de:Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein]]

[[File:酉の市Torinoichi000.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Torinoichi|de|Torinoichi|ja|酉の市|zh|酉の市}}の{{ill|Kumade|de|Kumade|ja|熊手|zh|熊手}}]] '''Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''' (鷲宮神社) is the name of a {{ill|shrine|de|Schrein|ja|神社|zh|神社}}.
It is a branch of the Ōtori faith based on {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|de|Ōtori-Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|zh|大鳥大社}}, though in many cases a Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine serves as the head shrine of the Kantō region’s {{ill|O‑Tori‑sama|de|O‑Tori‑sama|ja|お酉様|zh|O‑Tori‑sama}} cult – notably the one enshrined in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Präfektur Saitama|ja|埼玉県|zh|埼玉县}}’s {{ill|Kuki City|de|Kuki Stadt|ja|久喜市|zh|久喜市}}. The Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine in Kuki City was the head shrine (総社) of the {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|de|Fujiwara no Hidesato Ota-Clan|ja|太田氏|zh|太田氏|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato's|lt_ja=Fujiwara no Hidesato流太田氏|lt_de=Fujiwara no Hidesato Ota|lt_zh=Fujiwara no Hidesato流太田氏}} {{ill|Ota Villa|de|Ota-Anwesen|ja|太田荘|zh|太田荘}}, and many branch shrines exist mainly along the {{ill|Nakagawa|de|Nakagawa|ja|中川|zh|中川}} region. In other areas, however, Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines are merely branches of other Ōtori faith shrines and may have no connection to the Kuki City shrine.
In addition, along the western part of the {{ill|Arakawa River|de|Arakawa|ja|荒川 (関東)|zh|Arakawa River|lt=Arakawa|lt_ja=荒川|lt_de=Arakawa|lt_zh=Arakawa}} basin there is the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|de|Hikawa-Schrein|ja|氷川神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|氷川神社}}, along the {{ill|Moto-Arakawa|de|Moto-Arakawa|ja|元荒川|zh|元荒川}} basin there is the {{ill|Kuizu Shrine|de|Kuizu-Schrein|ja|久伊豆神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|久伊豆神社}}, and in the eastern and northern parts along the {{ill|Tone River|de|Tone Fluss|ja|利根川|zh|利根川}} and {{ill|Edogawa River|de|Edogawa-Fluss|ja|江戸川|zh|江戸川}} basins, many {{ill|Katori Shrine|de|Katori-Schrein|ja|香取神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|香取神社}} are found. Occasionally, merged shrines such as {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|鷲香取神社}} or {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|香取鷲神社}} appear. Furthermore, since the term “宮” means “shrine,” many places today label their shrines as {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|鷲神社}} or {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|日鷲神社}}, even when their old or common names remain Washi‑no‑Miya. For example, there is the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|鷲神社 (台東区)}}, in Taitō Ward, Tokyo (as inscribed on its torii plaque), the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|鷲神社 (土浦市)}}, in Tsuchiura City, Ibaraki, and the {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma City)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|日鷲神社}}, in Minamisoma City, Fukushima.
As a {{ill|religious corporation|de|Religiöse Körperschaft|ja|宗教法人|zh|宗教法人}}, there are nationwide 22 Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines and 2 Washi‑no‑Miya Associations.
See also {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|de|Ōtori-Schrein|ja|大鳥神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鳥神社}}, {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|鷲神社}}, {{ill|Great Washi Shrine|de|Großer Washi-Schrein|ja|大鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鷲神社}}, and {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|日鷲神社}}.

=== Saitama Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|de|Washi-Schrein (Saitama, Iwatsuki Sueta)|ja|鷲神社 (さいたま市岩槻区末田)|zh|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)}} – Enshrined at Sueta, Iwatsuki-ku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Aoge Gochū Shrine|de|Aoge Gochū-Schrein|ja|青毛五柱神社|zh|Aoge Gochū Shrine}} – Enshrined at Aoge, Kuki City. * {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine}} – Enshrined at Shōbu Town, Kuki City. It enshrines as its principal deities the mother {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|zh|Kushinadahime|lt=Inada‑hime|lt_ja=稲田姫命|lt_de=Inada‑hime|lt_zh=稻田姫命}}, also known as {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine|lt=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_ja=袋田大明神|lt_de=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_zh=Fukuroda Daimyōjin}}, the child {{ill|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|de|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|ja|建比良鳥命|zh|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt=Wuyi Tori no Mikoto|lt_ja=武夷鳥命|lt_de=Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt_zh=武夷鳥命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|de|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|zh|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|lt_de=Ōkuninushi|lt_zh=大国主}} (also known as {{ill|Tamatsukushi Shrine|de|Tamatsukushi-Schrein|ja|玉敷神社|zh|Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt=Kuizu Daimyōjin|lt_ja=久伊豆大明神|lt_de=Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt_zh=Kuizu Daimyōjin}}). * {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|Washi Katori Shrine}} – Enshrined at Uchimaki, Kasukabe City.
=== Tokyo Metropolis === * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|Washi Shrine (Taitō)}} – Enshrined at Senshuku, Taitō Ward.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:酉の市Torinoichi000.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Torinoichi|de|Torinoichi|ja|酉の市|zh|酉の市}}の{{ill|Kumade|de|Kumade|ja|熊手|zh|熊手}}]] '''Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''' (鷲宮神社) is the name of a {{ill|shrine|de|Schrein|ja|神社|zh|神社}}.
It is a branch of the Ōtori faith based on {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|de|Ōtori-Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|zh|大鳥大社}}, though in many cases a Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine serves as the head shrine of the Kantō region’s {{ill|O‑Tori‑sama|de|O‑Tori‑sama|ja|お酉様|zh|O‑Tori‑sama}} cult – notably the one enshrined in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Präfektur Saitama|ja|埼玉県|zh|埼玉县}}’s {{ill|Kuki City|de|Kuki Stadt|ja|久喜市|zh|久喜市}}. The Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine in Kuki City was the head shrine (総社) of the {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|de|Fujiwara no Hidesato Ota-Clan|ja|太田氏|zh|太田氏|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato's|lt_ja=Fujiwara no Hidesato流太田氏|lt_de=Fujiwara no Hidesato Ota|lt_zh=Fujiwara no Hidesato流太田氏}} {{ill|Ota Villa|de|Ota-Anwesen|ja|太田荘|zh|太田荘}}, and many branch shrines exist mainly along the {{ill|Nakagawa|de|Nakagawa|ja|中川|zh|中川}} region. In other areas, however, Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines are merely branches of other Ōtori faith shrines and may have no connection to the Kuki City shrine.
In addition, along the western part of the {{ill|Arakawa River|de|Arakawa|ja|荒川 (関東)|zh|Arakawa River|lt=Arakawa|lt_ja=荒川|lt_de=Arakawa|lt_zh=Arakawa}} basin there is the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|de|Hikawa-Schrein|ja|氷川神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|氷川神社}}, along the {{ill|Moto-Arakawa|de|Moto-Arakawa|ja|元荒川|zh|元荒川}} basin there is the {{ill|Kuizu Shrine|de|Kuizu-Schrein|ja|久伊豆神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|久伊豆神社}}, and in the eastern and northern parts along the {{ill|Tone River|de|Tone Fluss|ja|利根川|zh|利根川}} and {{ill|Edogawa River|de|Edogawa-Fluss|ja|江戸川|zh|江戸川}} basins, many {{ill|Katori Shrine|de|Katori-Schrein|ja|香取神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|香取神社}} are found. Occasionally, merged shrines such as {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|鷲香取神社}} or {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|香取鷲神社}} appear. Furthermore, since the term “宮” means “shrine,” many places today label their shrines as {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|鷲神社}} or {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|日鷲神社}}, even when their old or common names remain Washi‑no‑Miya. For example, there is the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|鷲神社 (台東区)}}, in Taitō Ward, Tokyo (as inscribed on its torii plaque), the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|鷲神社 (土浦市)}}, in Tsuchiura City, Ibaraki, and the {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma City)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|日鷲神社}}, in Minamisoma City, Fukushima.
As a {{ill|religious corporation|de|Religiöse Körperschaft|ja|宗教法人|zh|宗教法人}}, there are nationwide 22 Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines and 2 Washi‑no‑Miya Associations.
See also {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|de|Ōtori-Schrein|ja|大鳥神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鳥神社}}, {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|鷲神社}}, {{ill|Great Washi Shrine|de|Großer Washi-Schrein|ja|大鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鷲神社}}, and {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|日鷲神社}}.
== Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines ==
=== Fukushima Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Koriyama)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Koriyama)|ja|鷲宮神社 (郡山市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Koriyama)}} – Enshrined at Suginoiri, Yatagawa, Tamura‑chō, Koriyama City.
=== Ibaraki Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kawachi Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Kawachi-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (河内町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kawachi Town)}} – Enshrined at Kofabayashi, Kawachi‑chō, Inashiki District. The furigana for the corporation name is “ワシミヤジンジャ.”
=== Tochigi Prefecture === * '''{{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Toga Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Tōchigi, Toga-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (栃木市都賀町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Toga Town)}}''' ('''Shimotsukuni Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''') – Enshrined at Ienaka, Toga Town, Tōchigi City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Hakamori Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Tōchigi, Hakamori-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (栃木市箱森町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Hakamori Town)}} – Enshrined at Hakamori Town, Tōchigi City. <!--** {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Utsunomiya)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Utsunomiya)|ja|鷲宮神社 (宇都宮市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Utsunomiya)}} – Enshrined at Sarinotani, Utsunomiya City. Not a religious corporation, hence temporarily commented out --> * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Sakura)|ja|鷲宮神社 (さくら市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City)}} – Enshrined at Washiyado, Sakura City. <!--* {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City, Ujiie)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Sakura, Ujiie)|ja|鷲宮神社 (さくら市氏家)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City, Ujiie)}} – Enshrined at Ujiie, Sakura City. Not a religious corporation, hence temporarily commented out --> * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sano City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Sano)|ja|鷲宮神社 (佐野市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sano City)}} – Enshrined at Inubushi Kamimachi, Sano City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shimotsuke City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Shimotsuke)|ja|鷲宮神社 (下野市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shimotsuke City)}} – Enshrined at Hashimoto, Shimotsuke City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Ishijima)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Moka, Ishijima)|ja|鷲宮神社 (真岡市石島)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Ishijima)}} – Enshrined at Ishijima, Moka City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Washisu)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Moka, Washisu)|ja|鷲宮神社 (真岡市鷲巣)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Washisu)}} – Enshrined at Washisu, Moka City.
=== Chiba Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Chiba City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Chiba)|ja|鷲宮神社 (千葉市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Chiba City)}} – Enshrined at Daiganji-chō, Chūō-ku, Chiba City.
=== Saitama Prefecture === * '''{{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein|ja|鷲宮神社|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine}}''' ('''Musashi‑no‑Kuni Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''') – Enshrined at Washi‑no‑Miya, Kuki City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Association (Kuki City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Gesellschaft (Kuki)|ja|鷲宮社 (久喜市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Association (Kuki City)}} – Enshrined at Hachihō, Kuki City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kuki City, 713 Shimosayami)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Kuki, 713 Shimosayami)|ja|鷲宮神社 (久喜市下早見713)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kuki City, 713 Shimosayami)}} – Enshrined at address 713 Shimosayami, Kuki City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kuki City, 1766 Shimosayami)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Kuki, 1766 Shimosayami)|ja|鷲宮神社 (久喜市下早見1766)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kuki City, 1766 Shimosayami)}} – Enshrined at address 1766 Shimosayami, Kuki City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Saitama City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Saitama)|ja|鷲宮神社 (さいたま市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Saitama City)}} – Enshrined at Ueno, Iwatsuki-ku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kazo City, Hanazaki)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Kazo, Hanazaki)|ja|鷲宮神社 (加須市花崎)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kazo City, Hanazaki)}} – Enshrined at Hanazaki, Kazo City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kazo City, Hisashita)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Kazo, Hisashita)|ja|鷲宮神社 (加須市久下)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kazo City, Hisashita)}} – Enshrined at Hisashita, Kazo City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shiraoka City, Uenoda)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Shiraoka, Uenoda)|ja|鷲宮神社 (白岡市上野田)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shiraoka City, Uenoda)}} – Enshrined at Uenoda, Shiraoka City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shiraoka City, Shimonoda)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Shiraoka, Shimonoda)|ja|鷲宮神社 (白岡市下野田)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shiraoka City, Shimonoda)}} – Enshrined at Shimonoda, Shiraoka City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Hanyu City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Hanyu)|ja|鷲宮神社 (羽生市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Hanyu City)}} – Enshrined at Hideyasu, Hanyu City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Fukaya City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Fukaya)|ja|鷲宮神社 (深谷市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Fukaya City)}} – Enshrined at Takabata, Fukaya City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Miyashiro Town, Higashi‑Kumebara)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Miyashiro, Higashi‑Kumebara)|ja|鷲宮神社 (宮代町東粂原)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Miyashiro Town, Higashi‑Kumebara)}} – Enshrined at Higashi‑Kumebara, Miyashiro Town, Minami‑Saitama District. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Miyashiro Town, Nishi‑Kumebara)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Miyashiro, Nishi‑Kumebara)|ja|鷲宮神社 (宮代町西粂原)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Miyashiro Town, Nishi‑Kumebara)}} – Enshrined at Nishi‑Kumebara, Miyashiro Town, Minami‑Saitama District. The corporation name is “Religious Corporation Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine.”
=== Yamanashi Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Association (Nirasaki City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Gesellschaft (Nirasaki)|ja|鷲宮社 (韮崎市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Association (Nirasaki City)}} – Enshrined at Anayamachō, Nirasaki City.
== Alternate Names, Former Names, and Merged Shrines ==

=== Saitama Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|de|Washi-Schrein (Saitama, Iwatsuki Sueta)|ja|鷲神社 (さいたま市岩槻区末田)|zh|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)}} – Enshrined at Sueta, Iwatsuki-ku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Aoge Gochū Shrine|de|Aoge Gochū-Schrein|ja|青毛五柱神社|zh|Aoge Gochū Shrine}} – Enshrined at Aoge, Kuki City. * {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine}} – Enshrined at Shōbu Town, Kuki City. It enshrines as its principal deities the mother {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|zh|Kushinadahime|lt=Inada‑hime|lt_ja=稲田姫命|lt_de=Inada‑hime|lt_zh=稻田姫命}}, also known as {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine|lt=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_ja=袋田大明神|lt_de=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_zh=Fukuroda Daimyōjin}}, the child {{ill|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|de|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|ja|建比良鳥命|zh|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt=Wuyi Tori no Mikoto|lt_ja=武夷鳥命|lt_de=Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt_zh=武夷鳥命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|de|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|zh|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|lt_de=Ōkuninushi|lt_zh=大国主}} (also known as {{ill|Tamatsukushi Shrine|de|Tamatsukushi-Schrein|ja|玉敷神社|zh|Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt=Kuizu Daimyōjin|lt_ja=久伊豆大明神|lt_de=Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt_zh=Kuizu Daimyōjin}}). * {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|Washi Katori Shrine}} – Enshrined at Uchimaki, Kasukabe City.

[[category:completion tier 1]][[ja:鷲宮神社]][[de:Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein]]

[[File:酉の市Torinoichi000.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Torinoichi|de|Torinoichi|ja|酉の市|zh|酉の市}}の{{ill|Kumade|de|Kumade|ja|熊手|zh|熊手}}]] '''Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''' (鷲宮神社) is the name of a {{ill|shrine|de|Schrein|ja|神社|zh|神社}}.
It is a branch of the Ōtori faith based on {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|de|Ōtori-Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|zh|大鳥大社}}, though in many cases a Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine serves as the head shrine of the Kantō region’s {{ill|O‑Tori‑sama|de|O‑Tori‑sama|ja|お酉様|zh|O‑Tori‑sama}} cult – notably the one enshrined in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Präfektur Saitama|ja|埼玉県|zh|埼玉县}}’s {{ill|Kuki City|de|Kuki Stadt|ja|久喜市|zh|久喜市}}. The Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine in Kuki City was the head shrine (総社) of the {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|de|Fujiwara no Hidesato Ota-Clan|ja|太田氏|zh|太田氏|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato's|lt_ja=Fujiwara no Hidesato流太田氏|lt_de=Fujiwara no Hidesato Ota|lt_zh=Fujiwara no Hidesato流太田氏}} {{ill|Ota Villa|de|Ota-Anwesen|ja|太田荘|zh|太田荘}}, and many branch shrines exist mainly along the {{ill|Nakagawa|de|Nakagawa|ja|中川|zh|中川}} region. In other areas, however, Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines are merely branches of other Ōtori faith shrines and may have no connection to the Kuki City shrine.
In addition, along the western part of the {{ill|Arakawa River|de|Arakawa|ja|荒川 (関東)|zh|Arakawa River|lt=Arakawa|lt_ja=荒川|lt_de=Arakawa|lt_zh=Arakawa}} basin there is the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|de|Hikawa-Schrein|ja|氷川神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|氷川神社}}, along the {{ill|Moto-Arakawa|de|Moto-Arakawa|ja|元荒川|zh|元荒川}} basin there is the {{ill|Kuizu Shrine|de|Kuizu-Schrein|ja|久伊豆神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|久伊豆神社}}, and in the eastern and northern parts along the {{ill|Tone River|de|Tone Fluss|ja|利根川|zh|利根川}} and {{ill|Edogawa River|de|Edogawa-Fluss|ja|江戸川|zh|江戸川}} basins, many {{ill|Katori Shrine|de|Katori-Schrein|ja|香取神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|香取神社}} are found. Occasionally, merged shrines such as {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|鷲香取神社}} or {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|香取鷲神社}} appear. Furthermore, since the term “宮” means “shrine,” many places today label their shrines as {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|鷲神社}} or {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|日鷲神社}}, even when their old or common names remain Washi‑no‑Miya. For example, there is the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|鷲神社 (台東区)}}, in Taitō Ward, Tokyo (as inscribed on its torii plaque), the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|鷲神社 (土浦市)}}, in Tsuchiura City, Ibaraki, and the {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma City)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|日鷲神社}}, in Minamisoma City, Fukushima.
As a {{ill|religious corporation|de|Religiöse Körperschaft|ja|宗教法人|zh|宗教法人}}, there are nationwide 22 Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines and 2 Washi‑no‑Miya Associations.
See also {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|de|Ōtori-Schrein|ja|大鳥神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鳥神社}}, {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|鷲神社}}, {{ill|Great Washi Shrine|de|Großer Washi-Schrein|ja|大鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鷲神社}}, and {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|日鷲神社}}.
== Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines ==
=== Fukushima Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Koriyama)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Koriyama)|ja|鷲宮神社 (郡山市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Koriyama)}} – Enshrined at Suginoiri, Yatagawa, Tamura‑chō, Koriyama City.
=== Ibaraki Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kawachi Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Kawachi-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (河内町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kawachi Town)}} – Enshrined at Kofabayashi, Kawachi‑chō, Inashiki District. The furigana for the corporation name is “ワシミヤジンジャ.”
=== Tochigi Prefecture === * '''{{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Toga Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Tōchigi, Toga-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (栃木市都賀町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Toga Town)}}''' ('''Shimotsukuni Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''') – Enshrined at Ienaka, Toga Town, Tōchigi City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Hakamori Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Tōchigi, Hakamori-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (栃木市箱森町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Hakamori Town)}} – Enshrined at Hakamori Town, Tōchigi City. <!--** {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Utsunomiya)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Utsunomiya)|ja|鷲宮神社 (宇都宮市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Utsunomiya)}} – Enshrined at Sarinotani, Utsunomiya City. Not a religious corporation, hence temporarily commented out --> * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Sakura)|ja|鷲宮神社 (さくら市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City)}} – Enshrined at Washiyado, Sakura City. <!--* {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City, Ujiie)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Sakura, Ujiie)|ja|鷲宮神社 (さくら市氏家)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City, Ujiie)}} – Enshrined at Ujiie, Sakura City. Not a religious corporation, hence temporarily commented out --> * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sano City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Sano)|ja|鷲宮神社 (佐野市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sano City)}} – Enshrined at Inubushi Kamimachi, Sano City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shimotsuke City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Shimotsuke)|ja|鷲宮神社 (下野市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shimotsuke City)}} – Enshrined at Hashimoto, Shimotsuke City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Ishijima)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Moka, Ishijima)|ja|鷲宮神社 (真岡市石島)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Ishijima)}} – Enshrined at Ishijima, Moka City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Washisu)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Moka, Washisu)|ja|鷲宮神社 (真岡市鷲巣)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Washisu)}} – Enshrined at Washisu, Moka City.
=== Chiba Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Chiba City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Chiba)|ja|鷲宮神社 (千葉市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Chiba City)}} – Enshrined at Daiganji-chō, Chūō-ku, Chiba City.
=== Saitama Prefecture === * '''{{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein|ja|鷲宮神社|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine}}''' ('''Musashi‑no‑Kuni Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''') – Enshrined at Washi‑no‑Miya, Kuki City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Association (Kuki City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Gesellschaft (Kuki)|ja|鷲宮社 (久喜市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Association (Kuki City)}} – Enshrined at Hachihō, Kuki City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kuki City, 713 Shimosayami)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Kuki, 713 Shimosayami)|ja|鷲宮神社 (久喜市下早見713)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kuki City, 713 Shimosayami)}} – Enshrined at address 713 Shimosayami, Kuki City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kuki City, 1766 Shimosayami)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Kuki, 1766 Shimosayami)|ja|鷲宮神社 (久喜市下早見1766)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kuki City, 1766 Shimosayami)}} – Enshrined at address 1766 Shimosayami, Kuki City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Saitama City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Saitama)|ja|鷲宮神社 (さいたま市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Saitama City)}} – Enshrined at Ueno, Iwatsuki-ku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kazo City, Hanazaki)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Kazo, Hanazaki)|ja|鷲宮神社 (加須市花崎)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kazo City, Hanazaki)}} – Enshrined at Hanazaki, Kazo City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kazo City, Hisashita)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Kazo, Hisashita)|ja|鷲宮神社 (加須市久下)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kazo City, Hisashita)}} – Enshrined at Hisashita, Kazo City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shiraoka City, Uenoda)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Shiraoka, Uenoda)|ja|鷲宮神社 (白岡市上野田)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shiraoka City, Uenoda)}} – Enshrined at Uenoda, Shiraoka City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shiraoka City, Shimonoda)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Shiraoka, Shimonoda)|ja|鷲宮神社 (白岡市下野田)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shiraoka City, Shimonoda)}} – Enshrined at Shimonoda, Shiraoka City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Hanyu City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Hanyu)|ja|鷲宮神社 (羽生市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Hanyu City)}} – Enshrined at Hideyasu, Hanyu City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Fukaya City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Fukaya)|ja|鷲宮神社 (深谷市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Fukaya City)}} – Enshrined at Takabata, Fukaya City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Miyashiro Town, Higashi‑Kumebara)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Miyashiro, Higashi‑Kumebara)|ja|鷲宮神社 (宮代町東粂原)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Miyashiro Town, Higashi‑Kumebara)}} – Enshrined at Higashi‑Kumebara, Miyashiro Town, Minami‑Saitama District. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Miyashiro Town, Nishi‑Kumebara)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Miyashiro, Nishi‑Kumebara)|ja|鷲宮神社 (宮代町西粂原)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Miyashiro Town, Nishi‑Kumebara)}} – Enshrined at Nishi‑Kumebara, Miyashiro Town, Minami‑Saitama District. The corporation name is “Religious Corporation Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine.”
=== Yamanashi Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Association (Nirasaki City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Gesellschaft (Nirasaki)|ja|鷲宮社 (韮崎市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Association (Nirasaki City)}} – Enshrined at Anayamachō, Nirasaki City.
== Alternate Names, Former Names, and Merged Shrines ==
=== Fukushima Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)}} – Enshrined at Akechi, Onaba, Kodaka District, Minamisoma City.
=== Ibaraki Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|de|Sashinō-Schrein (Ishioka, Someya)|ja|佐志能神社 (石岡市染谷)|zh|Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)}} – Enshrined at Someya, Ishioka City. * {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|Katori Washi Shrine}} – Enshrined at Komagome, Koga City. * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura, Higashizakichō)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)}} – Enshrined at Higashizakichō, Tsuchiura City. * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Naka City)|de|Washi-Schrein (Naka)|ja|鷲神社 (那珂市)|zh|Washi Shrine (Naka City)}} – Enshrined at Washi‑no‑Miya, Hongosu, Naka City.
=== Saitama Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|de|Washi-Schrein (Saitama, Iwatsuki Sueta)|ja|鷲神社 (さいたま市岩槻区末田)|zh|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)}} – Enshrined at Sueta, Iwatsuki-ku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Aoge Gochū Shrine|de|Aoge Gochū-Schrein|ja|青毛五柱神社|zh|Aoge Gochū Shrine}} – Enshrined at Aoge, Kuki City. * {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine}} – Enshrined at Shōbu Town, Kuki City. It enshrines as its principal deities the mother {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|zh|Kushinadahime|lt=Inada‑hime|lt_ja=稲田姫命|lt_de=Inada‑hime|lt_zh=稻田姫命}}, also known as {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine|lt=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_ja=袋田大明神|lt_de=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_zh=Fukuroda Daimyōjin}}, the child {{ill|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|de|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|ja|建比良鳥命|zh|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt=Wuyi Tori no Mikoto|lt_ja=武夷鳥命|lt_de=Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt_zh=武夷鳥命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|de|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|zh|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|lt_de=Ōkuninushi|lt_zh=大国主}} (also known as {{ill|Tamatsukushi Shrine|de|Tamatsukushi-Schrein|ja|玉敷神社|zh|Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt=Kuizu Daimyōjin|lt_ja=久伊豆大明神|lt_de=Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt_zh=Kuizu Daimyōjin}}). * {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|Washi Katori Shrine}} – Enshrined at Uchimaki, Kasukabe City.
=== Tokyo Metropolis === * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|Washi Shrine (Taitō)}} – Enshrined at Senshuku, Taitō Ward.

== Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines ==

{{draft categories| [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines]] [[Category:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (disambiguation)]] [[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[en:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines]] [[sn:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (disambiguation)]]
[[category:completion tier 1]][[ja:鷲宮神社]][[de:Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein]]

[[File:酉の市Torinoichi000.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Torinoichi|de|Torinoichi|ja|酉の市|zh|酉の市}}の{{ill|Kumade|de|Kumade|ja|熊手|zh|熊手}}]] '''Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''' (鷲宮神社) is the name of a {{ill|shrine|de|Schrein|ja|神社|zh|神社}}.
It is a branch of the Ōtori faith based on {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|de|Ōtori-Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|zh|大鳥大社}}, though in many cases a Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine serves as the head shrine of the Kantō region’s {{ill|O‑Tori‑sama|de|O‑Tori‑sama|ja|お酉様|zh|O‑Tori‑sama}} cult – notably the one enshrined in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Präfektur Saitama|ja|埼玉県|zh|埼玉县}}’s {{ill|Kuki City|de|Kuki Stadt|ja|久喜市|zh|久喜市}}. The Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine in Kuki City was the head shrine (総社) of the {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|de|Fujiwara no Hidesato Ota-Clan|ja|太田氏|zh|太田氏|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato's|lt_ja=Fujiwara no Hidesato流太田氏|lt_de=Fujiwara no Hidesato Ota|lt_zh=Fujiwara no Hidesato流太田氏}} {{ill|Ota Villa|de|Ota-Anwesen|ja|太田荘|zh|太田荘}}, and many branch shrines exist mainly along the {{ill|Nakagawa|de|Nakagawa|ja|中川|zh|中川}} region. In other areas, however, Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines are merely branches of other Ōtori faith shrines and may have no connection to the Kuki City shrine.
In addition, along the western part of the {{ill|Arakawa River|de|Arakawa|ja|荒川 (関東)|zh|Arakawa River|lt=Arakawa|lt_ja=荒川|lt_de=Arakawa|lt_zh=Arakawa}} basin there is the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|de|Hikawa-Schrein|ja|氷川神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|氷川神社}}, along the {{ill|Moto-Arakawa|de|Moto-Arakawa|ja|元荒川|zh|元荒川}} basin there is the {{ill|Kuizu Shrine|de|Kuizu-Schrein|ja|久伊豆神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|久伊豆神社}}, and in the eastern and northern parts along the {{ill|Tone River|de|Tone Fluss|ja|利根川|zh|利根川}} and {{ill|Edogawa River|de|Edogawa-Fluss|ja|江戸川|zh|江戸川}} basins, many {{ill|Katori Shrine|de|Katori-Schrein|ja|香取神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|香取神社}} are found. Occasionally, merged shrines such as {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|鷲香取神社}} or {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|香取鷲神社}} appear. Furthermore, since the term “宮” means “shrine,” many places today label their shrines as {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|鷲神社}} or {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|日鷲神社}}, even when their old or common names remain Washi‑no‑Miya. For example, there is the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|鷲神社 (台東区)}}, in Taitō Ward, Tokyo (as inscribed on its torii plaque), the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|鷲神社 (土浦市)}}, in Tsuchiura City, Ibaraki, and the {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma City)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|日鷲神社}}, in Minamisoma City, Fukushima.
As a {{ill|religious corporation|de|Religiöse Körperschaft|ja|宗教法人|zh|宗教法人}}, there are nationwide 22 Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines and 2 Washi‑no‑Miya Associations.
See also {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|de|Ōtori-Schrein|ja|大鳥神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鳥神社}}, {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|鷲神社}}, {{ill|Great Washi Shrine|de|Großer Washi-Schrein|ja|大鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鷲神社}}, and {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|日鷲神社}}.

{{draft categories| [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines]] [[Category:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (disambiguation)]] [[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] }} [[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[en:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines]] [[sn:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (disambiguation)]]
[[category:completion tier 1]][[ja:鷲宮神社]][[de:Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein]]

[[File:酉の市Torinoichi000.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Torinoichi|de|Torinoichi|ja|酉の市|zh|酉の市}}の{{ill|Kumade|de|Kumade|ja|熊手|zh|熊手}}]] '''Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''' (鷲宮神社) is the name of a {{ill|shrine|de|Schrein|ja|神社|zh|神社}}.
It is a branch of the Ōtori faith based on {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|de|Ōtori-Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|zh|大鳥大社}}, though in many cases a Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine serves as the head shrine of the Kantō region’s {{ill|O‑Tori‑sama|de|O‑Tori‑sama|ja|お酉様|zh|O‑Tori‑sama}} cult – notably the one enshrined in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Präfektur Saitama|ja|埼玉県|zh|埼玉县}}’s {{ill|Kuki City|de|Kuki Stadt|ja|久喜市|zh|久喜市}}. The Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine in Kuki City was the head shrine (総社) of the {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|de|Fujiwara no Hidesato Ota-Clan|ja|太田氏|zh|太田氏|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato's|lt_ja=Fujiwara no Hidesato流太田氏|lt_de=Fujiwara no Hidesato Ota|lt_zh=Fujiwara no Hidesato流太田氏}} {{ill|Ota Villa|de|Ota-Anwesen|ja|太田荘|zh|太田荘}}, and many branch shrines exist mainly along the {{ill|Nakagawa|de|Nakagawa|ja|中川|zh|中川}} region. In other areas, however, Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines are merely branches of other Ōtori faith shrines and may have no connection to the Kuki City shrine.
In addition, along the western part of the {{ill|Arakawa River|de|Arakawa|ja|荒川 (関東)|zh|Arakawa River|lt=Arakawa|lt_ja=荒川|lt_de=Arakawa|lt_zh=Arakawa}} basin there is the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|de|Hikawa-Schrein|ja|氷川神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|氷川神社}}, along the {{ill|Moto-Arakawa|de|Moto-Arakawa|ja|元荒川|zh|元荒川}} basin there is the {{ill|Kuizu Shrine|de|Kuizu-Schrein|ja|久伊豆神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|久伊豆神社}}, and in the eastern and northern parts along the {{ill|Tone River|de|Tone Fluss|ja|利根川|zh|利根川}} and {{ill|Edogawa River|de|Edogawa-Fluss|ja|江戸川|zh|江戸川}} basins, many {{ill|Katori Shrine|de|Katori-Schrein|ja|香取神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|香取神社}} are found. Occasionally, merged shrines such as {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|鷲香取神社}} or {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|香取鷲神社}} appear. Furthermore, since the term “宮” means “shrine,” many places today label their shrines as {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|鷲神社}} or {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|日鷲神社}}, even when their old or common names remain Washi‑no‑Miya. For example, there is the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|鷲神社 (台東区)}}, in Taitō Ward, Tokyo (as inscribed on its torii plaque), the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|鷲神社 (土浦市)}}, in Tsuchiura City, Ibaraki, and the {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma City)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|日鷲神社}}, in Minamisoma City, Fukushima.
As a {{ill|religious corporation|de|Religiöse Körperschaft|ja|宗教法人|zh|宗教法人}}, there are nationwide 22 Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines and 2 Washi‑no‑Miya Associations.
See also {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|de|Ōtori-Schrein|ja|大鳥神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鳥神社}}, {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|鷲神社}}, {{ill|Great Washi Shrine|de|Großer Washi-Schrein|ja|大鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鷲神社}}, and {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|日鷲神社}}.

{{draft categories| [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines]] [[Category:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (disambiguation)]] [[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] }} [[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[en:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines]] [[sn:Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (disambiguation)]]
[[category:completion tier 1]][[ja:鷲宮神社]][[de:Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein]]

[[File:酉の市Torinoichi000.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Torinoichi|de|Torinoichi|ja|酉の市|zh|酉の市}}の{{ill|Kumade|de|Kumade|ja|熊手|zh|熊手}}]] '''Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''' (鷲宮神社) is the name of a {{ill|shrine|de|Schrein|ja|神社|zh|神社}}.
It is a branch of the Ōtori faith based on {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|de|Ōtori-Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|zh|大鳥大社}}, though in many cases a Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine serves as the head shrine of the Kantō region’s {{ill|O‑Tori‑sama|de|O‑Tori‑sama|ja|お酉様|zh|O‑Tori‑sama}} cult – notably the one enshrined in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Präfektur Saitama|ja|埼玉県|zh|埼玉县}}’s {{ill|Kuki City|de|Kuki Stadt|ja|久喜市|zh|久喜市}}. The Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine in Kuki City was the head shrine (総社) of the {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|de|Fujiwara no Hidesato Ota-Clan|ja|太田氏|zh|太田氏|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato's|lt_ja=Fujiwara no Hidesato流太田氏|lt_de=Fujiwara no Hidesato Ota|lt_zh=Fujiwara no Hidesato流太田氏}} {{ill|Ota Villa|de|Ota-Anwesen|ja|太田荘|zh|太田荘}}, and many branch shrines exist mainly along the {{ill|Nakagawa|de|Nakagawa|ja|中川|zh|中川}} region. In other areas, however, Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines are merely branches of other Ōtori faith shrines and may have no connection to the Kuki City shrine.
In addition, along the western part of the {{ill|Arakawa River|de|Arakawa|ja|荒川 (関東)|zh|Arakawa River|lt=Arakawa|lt_ja=荒川|lt_de=Arakawa|lt_zh=Arakawa}} basin there is the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|de|Hikawa-Schrein|ja|氷川神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|氷川神社}}, along the {{ill|Moto-Arakawa|de|Moto-Arakawa|ja|元荒川|zh|元荒川}} basin there is the {{ill|Kuizu Shrine|de|Kuizu-Schrein|ja|久伊豆神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|久伊豆神社}}, and in the eastern and northern parts along the {{ill|Tone River|de|Tone Fluss|ja|利根川|zh|利根川}} and {{ill|Edogawa River|de|Edogawa-Fluss|ja|江戸川|zh|江戸川}} basins, many {{ill|Katori Shrine|de|Katori-Schrein|ja|香取神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|香取神社}} are found. Occasionally, merged shrines such as {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|鷲香取神社}} or {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|香取鷲神社}} appear. Furthermore, since the term “宮” means “shrine,” many places today label their shrines as {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|鷲神社}} or {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|日鷲神社}}, even when their old or common names remain Washi‑no‑Miya. For example, there is the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|鷲神社 (台東区)}}, in Taitō Ward, Tokyo (as inscribed on its torii plaque), the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|鷲神社 (土浦市)}}, in Tsuchiura City, Ibaraki, and the {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma City)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|日鷲神社}}, in Minamisoma City, Fukushima.
As a {{ill|religious corporation|de|Religiöse Körperschaft|ja|宗教法人|zh|宗教法人}}, there are nationwide 22 Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines and 2 Washi‑no‑Miya Associations.
See also {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|de|Ōtori-Schrein|ja|大鳥神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鳥神社}}, {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|鷲神社}}, {{ill|Great Washi Shrine|de|Großer Washi-Schrein|ja|大鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鷲神社}}, and {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|日鷲神社}}.

[[File:酉の市Torinoichi000.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Torinoichi|de|Torinoichi|ja|酉の市|zh|酉の市}}の{{ill|Kumade|de|Kumade|ja|熊手|zh|熊手}}]] '''Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''' (鷲宮神社) is the name of a {{ill|shrine|de|Schrein|ja|神社|zh|神社}}.
It is a branch of the Ōtori faith based on {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|de|Ōtori-Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|zh|大鳥大社}}, though in many cases a Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine serves as the head shrine of the Kantō region’s {{ill|O‑Tori‑sama|de|O‑Tori‑sama|ja|お酉様|zh|O‑Tori‑sama}} cult – notably the one enshrined in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Präfektur Saitama|ja|埼玉県|zh|埼玉县}}’s {{ill|Kuki City|de|Kuki Stadt|ja|久喜市|zh|久喜市}}. The Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine in Kuki City was the head shrine (総社) of the {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|de|Fujiwara no Hidesato Ota-Clan|ja|太田氏|zh|太田氏|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato's|lt_ja=Fujiwara no Hidesato流太田氏|lt_de=Fujiwara no Hidesato Ota|lt_zh=Fujiwara no Hidesato流太田氏}} {{ill|Ota Villa|de|Ota-Anwesen|ja|太田荘|zh|太田荘}}, and many branch shrines exist mainly along the {{ill|Nakagawa|de|Nakagawa|ja|中川|zh|中川}} region. In other areas, however, Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines are merely branches of other Ōtori faith shrines and may have no connection to the Kuki City shrine.
In addition, along the western part of the {{ill|Arakawa River|de|Arakawa|ja|荒川 (関東)|zh|Arakawa River|lt=Arakawa|lt_ja=荒川|lt_de=Arakawa|lt_zh=Arakawa}} basin there is the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|de|Hikawa-Schrein|ja|氷川神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|氷川神社}}, along the {{ill|Moto-Arakawa|de|Moto-Arakawa|ja|元荒川|zh|元荒川}} basin there is the {{ill|Kuizu Shrine|de|Kuizu-Schrein|ja|久伊豆神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|久伊豆神社}}, and in the eastern and northern parts along the {{ill|Tone River|de|Tone Fluss|ja|利根川|zh|利根川}} and {{ill|Edogawa River|de|Edogawa-Fluss|ja|江戸川|zh|江戸川}} basins, many {{ill|Katori Shrine|de|Katori-Schrein|ja|香取神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|香取神社}} are found. Occasionally, merged shrines such as {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|鷲香取神社}} or {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|香取鷲神社}} appear. Furthermore, since the term “宮” means “shrine,” many places today label their shrines as {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|鷲神社}} or {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|日鷲神社}}, even when their old or common names remain Washi‑no‑Miya. For example, there is the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|鷲神社 (台東区)}}, in Taitō Ward, Tokyo (as inscribed on its torii plaque), the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|鷲神社 (土浦市)}}, in Tsuchiura City, Ibaraki, and the {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma City)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|日鷲神社}}, in Minamisoma City, Fukushima.
As a {{ill|religious corporation|de|Religiöse Körperschaft|ja|宗教法人|zh|宗教法人}}, there are nationwide 22 Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines and 2 Washi‑no‑Miya Associations.
See also {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|de|Ōtori-Schrein|ja|大鳥神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鳥神社}}, {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|鷲神社}}, {{ill|Great Washi Shrine|de|Großer Washi-Schrein|ja|大鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鷲神社}}, and {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|日鷲神社}}.
== Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines ==
=== Fukushima Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Koriyama)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Koriyama)|ja|鷲宮神社 (郡山市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Koriyama)}} – Enshrined at Suginoiri, Yatagawa, Tamura‑chō, Koriyama City.
=== Ibaraki Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kawachi Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Kawachi-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (河内町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kawachi Town)}} – Enshrined at Kofabayashi, Kawachi‑chō, Inashiki District. The furigana for the corporation name is “ワシミヤジンジャ.”
=== Tochigi Prefecture === * '''{{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Toga Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Tōchigi, Toga-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (栃木市都賀町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Toga Town)}}''' ('''Shimotsukuni Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''') – Enshrined at Ienaka, Toga Town, Tōchigi City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Hakamori Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Tōchigi, Hakamori-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (栃木市箱森町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Hakamori Town)}} – Enshrined at Hakamori Town, Tōchigi City. <!--** {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Utsunomiya)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Utsunomiya)|ja|鷲宮神社 (宇都宮市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Utsunomiya)}} – Enshrined at Sarinotani, Utsunomiya City. Not a religious corporation, hence temporarily commented out --> * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Sakura)|ja|鷲宮神社 (さくら市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City)}} – Enshrined at Washiyado, Sakura City. <!--* {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City, Ujiie)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Sakura, Ujiie)|ja|鷲宮神社 (さくら市氏家)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City, Ujiie)}} – Enshrined at Ujiie, Sakura City. Not a religious corporation, hence temporarily commented out --> * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sano City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Sano)|ja|鷲宮神社 (佐野市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sano City)}} – Enshrined at Inubushi Kamimachi, Sano City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shimotsuke City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Shimotsuke)|ja|鷲宮神社 (下野市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shimotsuke City)}} – Enshrined at Hashimoto, Shimotsuke City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Ishijima)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Moka, Ishijima)|ja|鷲宮神社 (真岡市石島)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Ishijima)}} – Enshrined at Ishijima, Moka City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Washisu)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Moka, Washisu)|ja|鷲宮神社 (真岡市鷲巣)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Washisu)}} – Enshrined at Washisu, Moka City.
=== Chiba Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Chiba City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Chiba)|ja|鷲宮神社 (千葉市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Chiba City)}} – Enshrined at Daiganji-chō, Chūō-ku, Chiba City.
=== Saitama Prefecture === * '''{{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein|ja|鷲宮神社|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine}}''' ('''Musashi‑no‑Kuni Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''') – Enshrined at Washi‑no‑Miya, Kuki City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Association (Kuki City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Gesellschaft (Kuki)|ja|鷲宮社 (久喜市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Association (Kuki City)}} – Enshrined at Hachihō, Kuki City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kuki City, 713 Shimosayami)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Kuki, 713 Shimosayami)|ja|鷲宮神社 (久喜市下早見713)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kuki City, 713 Shimosayami)}} – Enshrined at == Alternate Names, Former Names, and Merged Shrines ==
=== Fukushima Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)}} – Enshrined at Akechi, Onaba, Kodaka District, Minamisoma City.
=== Ibaraki Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|de|Sashinō-Schrein (Ishioka, Someya)|ja|佐志能神社 (石岡市染谷)|zh|Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)}} – Enshrined at Someya, Ishioka City. * {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|Katori Washi Shrine}} – Enshrined at Komagome, Koga City. * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura, Higashizakichō)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)}} – Enshrined at Higashizakichō, Tsuchiura City. * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Naka City)|de|Washi-Schrein (Naka)|ja|鷲神社 (那珂市)|zh|Washi Shrine (Naka City)}} – Enshrined at Washi‑no‑Miya, Hongosu, Naka City.
=== Saitama Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|de|Washi-Schrein (Saitama, Iwatsuki Sueta)|ja|鷲神社 (さいたま市岩槻区末田)|zh|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)}} – Enshrined at Sueta, Iwatsuki-ku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Aoge Gochū Shrine|de|Aoge Gochū-Schrein|ja|青毛五柱神社|zh|Aoge Gochū Shrine}} – Enshrined at Aoge, Kuki City. * {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine}} – Enshrined at Shōbu Town, Kuki City. It enshrines as its principal deities the mother {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|zh|Kushinadahime|lt=Inada‑hime|lt_ja=稲田姫命|lt_de=Inada‑hime|lt_zh=稻田姫命}}, also known as {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine|lt=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_ja=袋田大明神|lt_de=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_zh=Fukuroda Daimyōjin}}, the child {{ill|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|de|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|ja|建比良鳥命|zh|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt=Wuyi Tori no Mikoto|lt_ja=武夷鳥命|lt_de=Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt_zh=武夷鳥命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|de|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|zh|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|lt_de=Ōkuninushi|lt_zh=大国主}} (also known as {{ill|Tamatsukushi Shrine|de|Tamatsukushi-Schrein|ja|玉敷神社|zh|Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt=Kuizu Daimyōjin|lt_ja=久伊豆大明神|lt_de=Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt_zh=Kuizu Daimyōjin}}). * {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|Washi Katori Shrine}} – Enshrined at Uchimaki, Kasukabe City.
=== Tokyo Metropolis === * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|Washi Shrine (Taitō)}} – Enshrined at Senshuku, Taitō Ward.

It is a branch of the Ōtori faith based on {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|de|Ōtori-Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|zh|大鳥大社}}, though in many cases a Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine serves as the head shrine of the Kantō region’s {{ill|O‑Tori‑sama|de|O‑Tori‑sama|ja|お酉様|zh|O‑Tori‑sama}} cult – notably the one enshrined in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Präfektur Saitama|ja|埼玉県|zh|埼玉县}}’s {{ill|Kuki City|de|Kuki Stadt|ja|久喜市|zh|久喜市}}. The Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine in Kuki City was the head shrine (総社) of the {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|de|Fujiwara no Hidesato Ota-Clan|ja|太田氏|zh|太田氏|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato's|lt_ja=Fujiwara no Hidesato流太田氏|lt_de=Fujiwara no Hidesato Ota|lt_zh=Fujiwara no Hidesato流太田氏}} {{ill|Ota Villa|de|Ota-Anwesen|ja|太田荘|zh|太田荘}}, and many branch shrines exist mainly along the {{ill|Nakagawa|de|Nakagawa|ja|中川|zh|中川}} region. In other areas, however, Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines are merely branches of other Ōtori faith shrines and may have no connection to the Kuki City shrine.
In addition, along the western part of the {{ill|Arakawa River|de|Arakawa|ja|荒川 (関東)|zh|Arakawa River|lt=Arakawa|lt_ja=荒川|lt_de=Arakawa|lt_zh=Arakawa}} basin there is the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|de|Hikawa-Schrein|ja|氷川神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|氷川神社}}, along the {{ill|Moto-Arakawa|de|Moto-Arakawa|ja|元荒川|zh|元荒川}} basin there is the {{ill|Kuizu Shrine|de|Kuizu-Schrein|ja|久伊豆神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|久伊豆神社}}, and in the eastern and northern parts along the {{ill|Tone River|de|Tone Fluss|ja|利根川|zh|利根川}} and {{ill|Edogawa River|de|Edogawa-Fluss|ja|江戸川|zh|江戸川}} basins, many {{ill|Katori Shrine|de|Katori-Schrein|ja|香取神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|香取神社}} are found. Occasionally, merged shrines such as {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|鷲香取神社}} or {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|香取鷲神社}} appear. Furthermore, since the term “宮” means “shrine,” many places today label their shrines as {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社|zh|鷲神社}} or {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社|zh|日鷲神社}}, even when their old or common names remain Washi‑no‑Miya. For example, there is the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|鷲神社 (台東区)}}, in Taitō Ward, Tokyo (as inscribed on its torii plaque), the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|鷲神社 (土浦市)}}, in Tsuchiura City, Ibaraki, and the {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma City)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|日鷲神社}}, in Minamisoma City, Fukushima.
As a {{ill|religious corporation|de|Religiöse Körperschaft|ja|宗教法人|zh|宗教法人}}, there are nationwide 22 Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines and 2 Washi‑no‑Miya Associations.

== Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines ==
=== Fukushima Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Koriyama)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Koriyama)|ja|鷲宮神社 (郡山市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Koriyama)}} – Enshrined at Suginoiri, Yatagawa, Tamura‑chō, Koriyama City.
=== Ibaraki Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kawachi Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Kawachi-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (河内町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kawachi Town)}} – Enshrined at Kofabayashi, Kawachi‑chō, Inashiki District. The furigana for the corporation name is “ワシミヤジンジャ.”

=== Saitama Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|de|Washi-Schrein (Saitama, Iwatsuki Sueta)|ja|鷲神社 (さいたま市岩槻区末田)|zh|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)}} – Enshrined at Sueta, Iwatsuki-ku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Aoge Gochū Shrine|de|Aoge Gochū-Schrein|ja|青毛五柱神社|zh|Aoge Gochū Shrine}} – Enshrined at Aoge, Kuki City. * {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine}} – Enshrined at Shōbu Town, Kuki City. It enshrines as its principal deities the mother {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|zh|Kushinadahime|lt=Inada‑hime|lt_ja=稲田姫命|lt_de=Inada‑hime|lt_zh=稻田姫命}}, also known as {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine|lt=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_ja=袋田大明神|lt_de=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_zh=Fukuroda Daimyōjin}}, the child {{ill|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|de|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|ja|建比良鳥命|zh|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt=Wuyi Tori no Mikoto|lt_ja=武夷鳥命|lt_de=Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt_zh=武夷鳥命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|de|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|zh|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|lt_de=Ōkuninushi|lt_zh=大国主}} (also known as {{ill|Tamatsukushi Shrine|de|Tamatsukushi-Schrein|ja|玉敷神社|zh|Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt=Kuizu Daimyōjin|lt_ja=久伊豆大明神|lt_de=Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt_zh=Kuizu Daimyōjin}}). * {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|Washi Katori Shrine}} – Enshrined at Uchimaki, Kasukabe City.
=== Tokyo Metropolis === * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|Washi Shrine (Taitō)}} – Enshrined at Senshuku, Taitō Ward.
