== Alternate Names, Former Names, and Merged Shrines ==
=== Fukushima Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|1=Hiwashi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510871}} – Enshrined at Akechi, Onaba, Kodaka District, Minamisoma City.
=== Ibaraki Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|de|Sashinō-Schrein (Ishioka, Someya)|ja|佐志能神社 (石岡市染谷)|zh|Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|1=Sashino Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225838}} – Enshrined at Someya, Ishioka City. * {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|Katori Washi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Washi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Komagome, Koga City. * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura, Higashizakichō)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Higashizakichō, Tsuchiura City. * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Naka City)|de|Washi-Schrein (Naka)|ja|鷲神社 (那珂市)|zh|Washi Shrine (Naka City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Naka City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Washi‑no‑Miya, Hongosu, Naka City.
=== Saitama Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|de|Washi-Schrein (Saitama, Iwatsuki Sueta)|ja|鷲神社 (さいたま市岩槻区末田)|zh|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|1=Ōtori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116884577}} – Enshrined at Sueta, Iwatsuki-ku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Aoge Gochū Shrine|de|Aoge Gochū-Schrein|ja|青毛五柱神社|zh|Aoge Gochū Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoge Gochū Shrine|1=Ōgegohashira Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662567}} – Enshrined at Aoge, Kuki City. * {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Shōbu Town, Kuki City. It enshrines as its principal deities the mother [[:en:Kushinadahime|Inada‑hime]], also known as {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine|lt=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_ja=袋田大明神|lt_de=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_zh=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the child {{ill|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|de|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|zh|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt=Wuyi Tori no Mikoto|lt_ja=武夷鳥命|lt_de=Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt_zh=武夷鳥命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehira Tori no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}}, and [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ookuninushi]] (also known as {{ill|Tamatsukushi Shrine|de|Tamatsukushi-Schrein|ja|玉敷神社|zh|Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt=Kuizu Daimyōjin|lt_ja=久伊豆大明神|lt_de=Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt_zh=Kuizu Daimyōjin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsukushi Shrine|1=Tamashiki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572627}}). * {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|Washi Katori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q134886645}} – Enshrined at Uchimaki, Kasukabe City.
=== Tokyo Metropolis === * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Taitō)|1=Ōtori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676284}} – Enshrined at Senshuku, Taitō Ward.

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q65293873]] Wikidata name: Washinomiya Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:酉の市Torinoichi000.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Torinoichi|de|Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Torinoichi|ja|酉の市|zh|酉の市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinoichi|qq=}}の{{ill|Rake (tool)|en|Rake (tool)|WD=Q200822|lt=Kumade}}]] '''Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''' (鷲宮神社) is the name of a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}}.
It is a branch of the Ōtori faith based on {{ill|Ōtori taisha|en|Ōtori taisha|WD=Q705151|lt=Ōtori Taisha}}, though in many cases a Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine serves as the head shrine of the Kantō region’s {{ill|O‑Tori‑sama|de|O‑Tori‑sama|ja|お酉様|zh|O‑Tori‑sama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/O‑Tori‑sama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市|1=Tori no Ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643942}} cult – notably the one enshrined in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}’s {{ill|Kuki, Saitama|en|Kuki, Saitama|WD=Q47535|lt=Kuki City}}. The Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine in Kuki City was the head shrine (総社) of the {{ill|Ōta clan|en|Ōta clan|WD=Q1391922|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato's}} {{ill|Ota Villa|de|Ota-Anwesen|ja|太田荘|zh|太田荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota Villa|1=Ota Villa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444411}}, and many branch shrines exist mainly along the {{ill|Naka River (Saitama Tokyo)|en|Naka River (Saitama Tokyo)|WD=Q2613967|lt=Nakagawa}} region. In other areas, however, Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines are merely branches of other Ōtori faith shrines and may have no connection to the Kuki City shrine.
In addition, along the western part of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Kantō)|en|Arakawa River (Kantō)|WD=Q625465|lt=Arakawa}} basin there is the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|en|Hikawa Shrine|WD=Q5760456|lt=Hikawa Shrine}}, along the {{ill|Moto-Arakawa|de|Moto-Arakawa|ja|元荒川|zh|元荒川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Arakawa|1=Moto-Arakawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388047}} basin there is the {{ill|Kuizu Shrine|de|Kuizu-Schrein|ja|久伊豆神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|久伊豆神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuizu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久伊豆神社|1=Hisaizu-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55523154}}, and in the eastern and northern parts along the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River}} and {{ill|Edo River|en|Edo River|WD=Q1194505|lt=Edogawa River}} basins, many {{ill|Katori Jingu|lt=Katori Jingu|lt=Katori Shrine|de|Katori-Schrein|ja|香取神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 香取神社}} are found. Occasionally, merged shrines such as {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|鷲香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|香取鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Washi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} appear. Furthermore, since the term “宮” means “shrine,” many places today label their shrines as {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社|zh|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}} or {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社|zh|日鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine|1=Ame-no-Hiwashi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65270357}}, even when their old or common names remain Washi‑no‑Miya. For example, there is the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|鷲神社 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Taitō)|1=Ōtori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676284}}, in Taitō Ward, Tokyo (as inscribed on its torii plaque), the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|鷲神社 (土浦市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Tsuchiura City, Ibaraki, and the {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma City)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|日鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|1=Hiwashi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510871}}, in Minamisoma City, Fukushima.
As a {{ill|religious corporation|de|Religiöse Körperschaft|ja|宗教法人|zh|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/religious corporation|qq=}}, there are nationwide 22 Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines and 2 Washi‑no‑Miya Associations.
See also {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|de|Ōtori-Schrein|ja|大鳥神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社|zh|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}}, {{ill|Great Washi Shrine|de|Großer Washi-Schrein|ja|大鷲神社|zh|大鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Washi Shrine|1=Ōwashi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65258950}}, and {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社|zh|日鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine|1=Ame-no-Hiwashi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65270357}}.
== Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines ==
=== Fukushima Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Koriyama)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Koriyama)|ja|鷲宮神社 (郡山市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Koriyama)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Koriyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Suginoiri, Yatagawa, Tamura‑chō, Koriyama City.
=== Ibaraki Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kawachi Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Kawachi-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (河内町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kawachi Town)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kawachi Town)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Kofabayashi, Kawachi‑chō, Inashiki District. The furigana for the corporation name is “ワシミヤジンジャ.”
=== Tochigi Prefecture === * '''{{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Toga Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Tōchigi, Toga-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (栃木市都賀町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Toga Town)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Toga Town)|1=Washinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65516640}}''' ('''Shimotsukuni Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''') – Enshrined at Ienaka, Toga Town, Tōchigi City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Hakamori Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Tōchigi, Hakamori-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (栃木市箱森町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Hakamori Town)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Hakamori Town)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Hakamori Town, Tōchigi City.
* {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Sakura)|ja|鷲宮神社 (さくら市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Washiyado, Sakura City.

{{wikidata link|Q65293873}} [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q65293873]] Wikidata name: Washinomiya Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:酉の市Torinoichi000.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Torinoichi|de|Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Torinoichi|ja|酉の市|zh|酉の市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinoichi|qq=}}の{{ill|Rake (tool)|en|Rake (tool)|WD=Q200822|lt=Kumade}}]] '''Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''' (鷲宮神社) is the name of a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}}.
It is a branch of the Ōtori faith based on {{ill|Ōtori taisha|en|Ōtori taisha|WD=Q705151|lt=Ōtori Taisha}}, though in many cases a Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine serves as the head shrine of the Kantō region’s {{ill|O‑Tori‑sama|de|O‑Tori‑sama|ja|お酉様|zh|O‑Tori‑sama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/O‑Tori‑sama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市|1=Tori no Ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643942}} cult – notably the one enshrined in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}’s {{ill|Kuki, Saitama|en|Kuki, Saitama|WD=Q47535|lt=Kuki City}}. The Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine in Kuki City was the head shrine (総社) of the {{ill|Ōta clan|en|Ōta clan|WD=Q1391922|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato's}} {{ill|Ota Villa|de|Ota-Anwesen|ja|太田荘|zh|太田荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota Villa|1=Ota Villa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444411}}, and many branch shrines exist mainly along the {{ill|Naka River (Saitama Tokyo)|en|Naka River (Saitama Tokyo)|WD=Q2613967|lt=Nakagawa}} region. In other areas, however, Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines are merely branches of other Ōtori faith shrines and may have no connection to the Kuki City shrine.
In addition, along the western part of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Kantō)|en|Arakawa River (Kantō)|WD=Q625465|lt=Arakawa}} basin there is the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|en|Hikawa Shrine|WD=Q5760456|lt=Hikawa Shrine}}, along the {{ill|Moto-Arakawa|de|Moto-Arakawa|ja|元荒川|zh|元荒川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Arakawa|1=Moto-Arakawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388047}} basin there is the {{ill|Kuizu Shrine|de|Kuizu-Schrein|ja|久伊豆神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|久伊豆神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuizu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久伊豆神社|1=Hisaizu-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55523154}}, and in the eastern and northern parts along the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River}} and {{ill|Edo River|en|Edo River|WD=Q1194505|lt=Edogawa River}} basins, many {{ill|Katori Jingū|lt=Katori Jingū|lt=Katori Shrine|de|Katori-Schrein|ja|香取神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 香取神社}} are found. Occasionally, merged shrines such as {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|鷲香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|香取鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Washi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} appear. Furthermore, since the term “宮” means “shrine,” many places today label their shrines as {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社|zh|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}} or {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社|zh|日鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine|1=Ame-no-Hiwashi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65270357}}, even when their old or common names remain Washi‑no‑Miya. For example, there is the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|鷲神社 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Taitō)|1=Ōtori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676284}}, in Taitō Ward, Tokyo (as inscribed on its torii plaque), the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|鷲神社 (土浦市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Tsuchiura City, Ibaraki, and the {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma City)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|日鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|1=Hiwashi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510871}}, in Minamisoma City, Fukushima.
As a {{ill|religious corporation|de|Religiöse Körperschaft|ja|宗教法人|zh|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/religious corporation|qq=}}, there are nationwide 22 Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines and 2 Washi‑no‑Miya Associations.
See also {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|de|Ōtori-Schrein|ja|大鳥神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社|zh|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}}, {{ill|Great Washi Shrine|de|Großer Washi-Schrein|ja|大鷲神社|zh|大鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Washi Shrine|1=Ōwashi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65258950}}, and {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社|zh|日鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine|1=Ame-no-Hiwashi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65270357}}.

=== Ibaraki Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|de|Sashinō-Schrein (Ishioka, Someya)|ja|佐志能神社 (石岡市染谷)|zh|Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|1=Sashino Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225838}} – Enshrined at Someya, Ishioka City. * {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|Katori Washi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Washi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Komagome, Koga City. * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura, Higashizakichō)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Higashizakichō, Tsuchiura City. * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Naka City)|de|Washi-Schrein (Naka)|ja|鷲神社 (那珂市)|zh|Washi Shrine (Naka City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Naka City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Washi‑no‑Miya, Hongosu, Naka City.
=== Saitama Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|de|Washi-Schrein (Saitama, Iwatsuki Sueta)|ja|鷲神社 (さいたま市岩槻区末田)|zh|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|1=Ōtori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116884577}} – Enshrined at Sueta, Iwatsuki-ku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Aoge Gochū Shrine|de|Aoge Gochū-Schrein|ja|青毛五柱神社|zh|Aoge Gochū Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoge Gochū Shrine|1=Ōgegohashira Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662567}} – Enshrined at Aoge, Kuki City. * {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Shōbu Town, Kuki City. It enshrines as its principal deities the mother {{ill|Kushinadahime|en|Kushinadahime|WD=Q505469|lt=Inada‑hime}}, also known as {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine|lt=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_ja=袋田大明神|lt_de=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_zh=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the child {{ill|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|de|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|zh|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt=Wuyi Tori no Mikoto|lt_ja=武夷鳥命|lt_de=Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt_zh=武夷鳥命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehira Tori no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ookuninushi}} (also known as {{ill|Tamatsukushi Shrine|de|Tamatsukushi-Schrein|ja|玉敷神社|zh|Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt=Kuizu Daimyōjin|lt_ja=久伊豆大明神|lt_de=Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt_zh=Kuizu Daimyōjin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsukushi Shrine|1=Tamashiki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572627}}). * {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|Washi Katori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q134886645}} – Enshrined at Uchimaki, Kasukabe City.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q65293873]] Wikidata name: Washinomiya Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:酉の市Torinoichi000.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Torinoichi|de|Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Torinoichi|ja|酉の市|zh|酉の市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinoichi|qq=}}の{{ill|Rake (tool)|en|Rake (tool)|WD=Q200822|lt=Kumade}}]] '''Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''' (鷲宮神社) is the name of a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}}.
It is a branch of the Ōtori faith based on {{ill|Ōtori taisha|en|Ōtori taisha|WD=Q705151|lt=Ōtori Taisha}}, though in many cases a Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine serves as the head shrine of the Kantō region’s {{ill|O‑Tori‑sama|de|O‑Tori‑sama|ja|お酉様|zh|O‑Tori‑sama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/O‑Tori‑sama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市|1=Tori no Ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643942}} cult – notably the one enshrined in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}’s {{ill|Kuki, Saitama|en|Kuki, Saitama|WD=Q47535|lt=Kuki City}}. The Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine in Kuki City was the head shrine (総社) of the {{ill|Ōta clan|en|Ōta clan|WD=Q1391922|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato's}} {{ill|Ota Villa|de|Ota-Anwesen|ja|太田荘|zh|太田荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota Villa|1=Ota Villa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444411}}, and many branch shrines exist mainly along the {{ill|Naka River (Saitama Tokyo)|en|Naka River (Saitama Tokyo)|WD=Q2613967|lt=Nakagawa}} region. In other areas, however, Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines are merely branches of other Ōtori faith shrines and may have no connection to the Kuki City shrine.
In addition, along the western part of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Kantō)|en|Arakawa River (Kantō)|WD=Q625465|lt=Arakawa}} basin there is the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|en|Hikawa Shrine|WD=Q5760456|lt=Hikawa Shrine}}, along the {{ill|Moto-Arakawa|de|Moto-Arakawa|ja|元荒川|zh|元荒川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Arakawa|1=Moto-Arakawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388047}} basin there is the {{ill|Kuizu Shrine|de|Kuizu-Schrein|ja|久伊豆神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|久伊豆神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuizu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久伊豆神社|1=Hisaizu-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55523154}}, and in the eastern and northern parts along the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River}} and {{ill|Edo River|en|Edo River|WD=Q1194505|lt=Edogawa River}} basins, many {{ill|Katori Jingū|lt=Katori Jingū|lt=Katori Shrine|de|Katori-Schrein|ja|香取神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 香取神社}} are found. Occasionally, merged shrines such as {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|鷲香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|香取鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Washi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} appear. Furthermore, since the term “宮” means “shrine,” many places today label their shrines as {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社|zh|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}} or {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社|zh|日鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine|1=Ame-no-Hiwashi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65270357}}, even when their old or common names remain Washi‑no‑Miya. For example, there is the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|鷲神社 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Taitō)|1=Ōtori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676284}}, in Taitō Ward, Tokyo (as inscribed on its torii plaque), the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|鷲神社 (土浦市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Tsuchiura City, Ibaraki, and the {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma City)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|日鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|1=Hiwashi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510871}}, in Minamisoma City, Fukushima.
As a {{ill|religious corporation|de|Religiöse Körperschaft|ja|宗教法人|zh|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/religious corporation|qq=}}, there are nationwide 22 Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines and 2 Washi‑no‑Miya Associations.
See also {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|de|Ōtori-Schrein|ja|大鳥神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社|zh|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}}, {{ill|Great Washi Shrine|de|Großer Washi-Schrein|ja|大鷲神社|zh|大鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Washi Shrine|1=Ōwashi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65258950}}, and {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社|zh|日鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine|1=Ame-no-Hiwashi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65270357}}.

== Alternate Names, Former Names, and Merged Shrines ==

=== Ibaraki Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|de|Sashinō-Schrein (Ishioka, Someya)|ja|佐志能神社 (石岡市染谷)|zh|Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|1=Sashino Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225838}} – Enshrined at Someya, Ishioka City. * {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|Katori Washi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Washi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Komagome, Koga City. * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura, Higashizakichō)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Higashizakichō, Tsuchiura City. * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Naka City)|de|Washi-Schrein (Naka)|ja|鷲神社 (那珂市)|zh|Washi Shrine (Naka City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Naka City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Washi‑no‑Miya, Hongosu, Naka City.
=== Saitama Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|de|Washi-Schrein (Saitama, Iwatsuki Sueta)|ja|鷲神社 (さいたま市岩槻区末田)|zh|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|1=Ōtori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116884577}} – Enshrined at Sueta, Iwatsuki-ku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Aoge Gochū Shrine|de|Aoge Gochū-Schrein|ja|青毛五柱神社|zh|Aoge Gochū Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoge Gochū Shrine|1=Ōgegohashira Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662567}} – Enshrined at Aoge, Kuki City. * {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Shōbu Town, Kuki City. It enshrines as its principal deities the mother {{ill|Kushinadahime|en|Kushinadahime|WD=Q505469|lt=Inada‑hime}}, also known as {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine|lt=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_ja=袋田大明神|lt_de=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_zh=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the child {{ill|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|de|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|zh|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt=Wuyi Tori no Mikoto|lt_ja=武夷鳥命|lt_de=Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt_zh=武夷鳥命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehira Tori no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ookuninushi}} (also known as {{ill|Tamatsukushi Shrine|de|Tamatsukushi-Schrein|ja|玉敷神社|zh|Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt=Kuizu Daimyōjin|lt_ja=久伊豆大明神|lt_de=Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt_zh=Kuizu Daimyōjin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsukushi Shrine|1=Tamashiki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572627}}). * {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|Washi Katori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q134886645}} – Enshrined at Uchimaki, Kasukabe City.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q65293873]] Wikidata name: Washinomiya Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:酉の市Torinoichi000.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Torinoichi|de|Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Torinoichi|ja|酉の市|zh|酉の市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinoichi|qq=}}の{{ill|Rake (tool)|en|Rake (tool)|WD=Q200822|lt=Kumade}}]] '''Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''' (鷲宮神社) is the name of a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}}.
It is a branch of the Ōtori faith based on {{ill|Ōtori taisha|en|Ōtori taisha|WD=Q705151|lt=Ōtori Taisha}}, though in many cases a Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine serves as the head shrine of the Kantō region’s {{ill|O‑Tori‑sama|de|O‑Tori‑sama|ja|お酉様|zh|O‑Tori‑sama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/O‑Tori‑sama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市|1=Tori no Ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643942}} cult – notably the one enshrined in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}’s {{ill|Kuki, Saitama|en|Kuki, Saitama|WD=Q47535|lt=Kuki City}}. The Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine in Kuki City was the head shrine (総社) of the {{ill|Ōta clan|en|Ōta clan|WD=Q1391922|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato's}} {{ill|Ota Villa|de|Ota-Anwesen|ja|太田荘|zh|太田荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota Villa|1=Ota Villa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444411}}, and many branch shrines exist mainly along the {{ill|Naka River (Saitama Tokyo)|en|Naka River (Saitama Tokyo)|WD=Q2613967|lt=Nakagawa}} region. In other areas, however, Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines are merely branches of other Ōtori faith shrines and may have no connection to the Kuki City shrine.
In addition, along the western part of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Kantō)|en|Arakawa River (Kantō)|WD=Q625465|lt=Arakawa}} basin there is the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|en|Hikawa Shrine|WD=Q5760456|lt=Hikawa Shrine}}, along the {{ill|Moto-Arakawa|de|Moto-Arakawa|ja|元荒川|zh|元荒川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Arakawa|1=Moto-Arakawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388047}} basin there is the {{ill|Kuizu Shrine|de|Kuizu-Schrein|ja|久伊豆神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|久伊豆神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuizu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久伊豆神社|1=Hisaizu-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55523154}}, and in the eastern and northern parts along the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River}} and {{ill|Edo River|en|Edo River|WD=Q1194505|lt=Edogawa River}} basins, many {{ill|Katori Jingū|lt=Katori Jingū|lt=Katori Shrine|de|Katori-Schrein|ja|香取神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 香取神社}} are found. Occasionally, merged shrines such as {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|鷲香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|香取鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Washi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} appear. Furthermore, since the term “宮” means “shrine,” many places today label their shrines as {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社|zh|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}} or {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社|zh|日鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine|1=Ame-no-Hiwashi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65270357}}, even when their old or common names remain Washi‑no‑Miya. For example, there is the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|鷲神社 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Taitō)|1=Ōtori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676284}}, in Taitō Ward, Tokyo (as inscribed on its torii plaque), the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|鷲神社 (土浦市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Tsuchiura City, Ibaraki, and the {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma City)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|日鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|1=Hiwashi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510871}}, in Minamisoma City, Fukushima.
As a {{ill|religious corporation|de|Religiöse Körperschaft|ja|宗教法人|zh|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/religious corporation|qq=}}, there are nationwide 22 Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines and 2 Washi‑no‑Miya Associations.
See also {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|de|Ōtori-Schrein|ja|大鳥神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社|zh|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}}, {{ill|Great Washi Shrine|de|Großer Washi-Schrein|ja|大鷲神社|zh|大鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Washi Shrine|1=Ōwashi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65258950}}, and {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社|zh|日鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine|1=Ame-no-Hiwashi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65270357}}.

== Alternate Names, Former Names, and Merged Shrines ==
=== Fukushima Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|1=Hiwashi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510871}} – Enshrined at Akechi, Onaba, Kodaka District, Minamisoma City.
=== Ibaraki Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|de|Sashinō-Schrein (Ishioka, Someya)|ja|佐志能神社 (石岡市染谷)|zh|Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|1=Sashino Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225838}} – Enshrined at Someya, Ishioka City. * {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|Katori Washi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Washi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Komagome, Koga City. * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura, Higashizakichō)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Higashizakichō, Tsuchiura City. * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Naka City)|de|Washi-Schrein (Naka)|ja|鷲神社 (那珂市)|zh|Washi Shrine (Naka City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Naka City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Washi‑no‑Miya, Hongosu, Naka City.
=== Saitama Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|de|Washi-Schrein (Saitama, Iwatsuki Sueta)|ja|鷲神社 (さいたま市岩槻区末田)|zh|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|1=Ōtori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116884577}} – Enshrined at Sueta, Iwatsuki-ku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Aoge Gochū Shrine|de|Aoge Gochū-Schrein|ja|青毛五柱神社|zh|Aoge Gochū Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoge Gochū Shrine|1=Ōgegohashira Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662567}} – Enshrined at Aoge, Kuki City. * {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Shōbu Town, Kuki City. It enshrines as its principal deities the mother {{ill|Kushinadahime|en|Kushinadahime|WD=Q505469|lt=Inada‑hime}}, also known as {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine|lt=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_ja=袋田大明神|lt_de=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_zh=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the child {{ill|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|de|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|zh|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt=Wuyi Tori no Mikoto|lt_ja=武夷鳥命|lt_de=Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt_zh=武夷鳥命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehira Tori no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ookuninushi}} (also known as {{ill|Tamatsukushi Shrine|de|Tamatsukushi-Schrein|ja|玉敷神社|zh|Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt=Kuizu Daimyōjin|lt_ja=久伊豆大明神|lt_de=Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt_zh=Kuizu Daimyōjin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsukushi Shrine|1=Tamashiki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572627}}). * {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|Washi Katori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q134886645}} – Enshrined at Uchimaki, Kasukabe City.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]]
[[File:酉の市Torinoichi000.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Torinoichi|de|Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Torinoichi|ja|酉の市|zh|酉の市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinoichi|qq=}}の{{ill|Rake (tool)|en|Rake (tool)|WD=Q200822|lt=Kumade}}]] '''Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''' (鷲宮神社) is the name of a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}}.
It is a branch of the Ōtori faith based on {{ill|Ōtori taisha|en|Ōtori taisha|WD=Q705151|lt=Ōtori Taisha}}, though in many cases a Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine serves as the head shrine of the Kantō region’s {{ill|O‑Tori‑sama|de|O‑Tori‑sama|ja|お酉様|zh|O‑Tori‑sama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/O‑Tori‑sama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市|1=Tori no Ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643942}} cult – notably the one enshrined in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}’s {{ill|Kuki, Saitama|en|Kuki, Saitama|WD=Q47535|lt=Kuki City}}. The Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine in Kuki City was the head shrine (総社) of the {{ill|Ōta clan|en|Ōta clan|WD=Q1391922|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato's}} {{ill|Ota Villa|de|Ota-Anwesen|ja|太田荘|zh|太田荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota Villa|1=Ota Villa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444411}}, and many branch shrines exist mainly along the {{ill|Naka River (Saitama Tokyo)|en|Naka River (Saitama Tokyo)|WD=Q2613967|lt=Nakagawa}} region. In other areas, however, Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines are merely branches of other Ōtori faith shrines and may have no connection to the Kuki City shrine.
In addition, along the western part of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Kantō)|en|Arakawa River (Kantō)|WD=Q625465|lt=Arakawa}} basin there is the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|en|Hikawa Shrine|WD=Q5760456|lt=Hikawa Shrine}}, along the {{ill|Moto-Arakawa|de|Moto-Arakawa|ja|元荒川|zh|元荒川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Arakawa|1=Moto-Arakawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388047}} basin there is the {{ill|Kuizu Shrine|de|Kuizu-Schrein|ja|久伊豆神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|久伊豆神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuizu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久伊豆神社|1=Hisaizu-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55523154}}, and in the eastern and northern parts along the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River}} and {{ill|Edo River|en|Edo River|WD=Q1194505|lt=Edogawa River}} basins, many {{ill|Katori Jingū|lt=Katori Jingū|lt=Katori Shrine|de|Katori-Schrein|ja|香取神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 香取神社}} are found. Occasionally, merged shrines such as {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|鷲香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|香取鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Washi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} appear. Furthermore, since the term “宮” means “shrine,” many places today label their shrines as {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社|zh|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}} or {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社|zh|日鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine|1=Ame-no-Hiwashi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65270357}}, even when their old or common names remain Washi‑no‑Miya. For example, there is the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|鷲神社 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Taitō)|1=Ōtori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676284}}, in Taitō Ward, Tokyo (as inscribed on its torii plaque), the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|鷲神社 (土浦市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Tsuchiura City, Ibaraki, and the {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma City)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|日鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|1=Hiwashi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510871}}, in Minamisoma City, Fukushima.
As a {{ill|religious corporation|de|Religiöse Körperschaft|ja|宗教法人|zh|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/religious corporation|qq=}}, there are nationwide 22 Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines and 2 Washi‑no‑Miya Associations.
See also {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|de|Ōtori-Schrein|ja|大鳥神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社|zh|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}}, {{ill|Great Washi Shrine|de|Großer Washi-Schrein|ja|大鷲神社|zh|大鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Washi Shrine|1=Ōwashi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65258950}}, and {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社|zh|日鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine|1=Ame-no-Hiwashi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65270357}}.
== Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines ==
=== Fukushima Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Koriyama)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Koriyama)|ja|鷲宮神社 (郡山市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Koriyama)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Koriyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Suginoiri, Yatagawa, Tamura‑chō, Koriyama City.
=== Ibaraki Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kawachi Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Kawachi-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (河内町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kawachi Town)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kawachi Town)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Kofabayashi, Kawachi‑chō, Inashiki District. The furigana for the corporation name is “ワシミヤジンジャ.”
=== Tochigi Prefecture === * '''{{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Toga Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Tōchigi, Toga-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (栃木市都賀町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Toga Town)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Toga Town)|1=Washinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65516640}}''' ('''Shimotsukuni Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''') – Enshrined at Ienaka, Toga Town, Tōchigi City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Hakamori Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Tōchigi, Hakamori-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (栃木市箱森町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Hakamori Town)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Hakamori Town)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Hakamori Town, Tōchigi City.
* {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Sakura)|ja|鷲宮神社 (さくら市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Washiyado, Sakura City.
* {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sano City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Sano)|ja|鷲宮神社 (佐野市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sano City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sano City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Inubushi Kamimachi, Sano City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shimotsuke City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Shimotsuke)|ja|鷲宮神社 (下野市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shimotsuke City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shimotsuke City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Hashimoto, Shimotsuke City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Ishijima)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Moka, Ishijima)|ja|鷲宮神社 (真岡市石島)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Ishijima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Ishijima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Ishijima, Moka City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Washisu)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Moka, Washisu)|ja|鷲宮神社 (真岡市鷲巣)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Washisu)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Washisu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Washisu, Moka City.
=== Chiba Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Chiba City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Chiba)|ja|鷲宮神社 (千葉市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Chiba City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Chiba City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Daiganji-chō, Chūō-ku, Chiba City.

== Alternate Names, Former Names, and Merged Shrines ==
=== Fukushima Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|1=Hiwashi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510871}} – Enshrined at Akechi, Onaba, Kodaka District, Minamisoma City.
=== Ibaraki Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|de|Sashinō-Schrein (Ishioka, Someya)|ja|佐志能神社 (石岡市染谷)|zh|Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|1=Sashino Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225838}} – Enshrined at Someya, Ishioka City. * {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|Katori Washi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Washi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Komagome, Koga City. * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura, Higashizakichō)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Higashizakichō, Tsuchiura City. * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Naka City)|de|Washi-Schrein (Naka)|ja|鷲神社 (那珂市)|zh|Washi Shrine (Naka City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Naka City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Washi‑no‑Miya, Hongosu, Naka City.
=== Saitama Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|de|Washi-Schrein (Saitama, Iwatsuki Sueta)|ja|鷲神社 (さいたま市岩槻区末田)|zh|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|1=Ōtori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116884577}} – Enshrined at Sueta, Iwatsuki-ku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Aoge Gochū Shrine|de|Aoge Gochū-Schrein|ja|青毛五柱神社|zh|Aoge Gochū Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoge Gochū Shrine|1=Ōgegohashira Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662567}} – Enshrined at Aoge, Kuki City. * {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Shōbu Town, Kuki City. It enshrines as its principal deities the mother {{ill|Kushinadahime|en|Kushinadahime|WD=Q505469|lt=Inada‑hime}}, also known as {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine|lt=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_ja=袋田大明神|lt_de=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_zh=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the child {{ill|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|de|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|zh|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt=Wuyi Tori no Mikoto|lt_ja=武夷鳥命|lt_de=Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt_zh=武夷鳥命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehira Tori no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ookuninushi}} (also known as {{ill|Tamatsukushi Shrine|de|Tamatsukushi-Schrein|ja|玉敷神社|zh|Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt=Kuizu Daimyōjin|lt_ja=久伊豆大明神|lt_de=Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt_zh=Kuizu Daimyōjin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsukushi Shrine|1=Tamashiki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572627}}). * {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|Washi Katori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q134886645}} – Enshrined at Uchimaki, Kasukabe City.
=== Tokyo Metropolis === * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Taitō)|1=Ōtori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676284}} – Enshrined at Senshuku, Taitō Ward.
== References == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]]
[[File:酉の市Torinoichi000.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Torinoichi|de|Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Torinoichi|ja|酉の市|zh|酉の市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinoichi|qq=}}の{{ill|Rake (tool)|en|Rake (tool)|WD=Q200822|lt=Kumade}}]] '''Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''' (鷲宮神社) is the name of a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}}.
It is a branch of the Ōtori faith based on {{ill|Ōtori taisha|en|Ōtori taisha|WD=Q705151|lt=Ōtori Taisha}}, though in many cases a Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine serves as the head shrine of the Kantō region’s {{ill|O‑Tori‑sama|de|O‑Tori‑sama|ja|お酉様|zh|O‑Tori‑sama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/O‑Tori‑sama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市|1=Tori no Ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643942}} cult – notably the one enshrined in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}’s {{ill|Kuki, Saitama|en|Kuki, Saitama|WD=Q47535|lt=Kuki City}}. The Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine in Kuki City was the head shrine (総社) of the {{ill|Ōta clan|en|Ōta clan|WD=Q1391922|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato's}} {{ill|Ota Villa|de|Ota-Anwesen|ja|太田荘|zh|太田荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota Villa|1=Ota Villa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444411}}, and many branch shrines exist mainly along the {{ill|Naka River (Saitama Tokyo)|en|Naka River (Saitama Tokyo)|WD=Q2613967|lt=Nakagawa}} region. In other areas, however, Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines are merely branches of other Ōtori faith shrines and may have no connection to the Kuki City shrine.
In addition, along the western part of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Kantō)|en|Arakawa River (Kantō)|WD=Q625465|lt=Arakawa}} basin there is the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|en|Hikawa Shrine|WD=Q5760456|lt=Hikawa Shrine}}, along the {{ill|Moto-Arakawa|de|Moto-Arakawa|ja|元荒川|zh|元荒川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Arakawa|1=Moto-Arakawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388047}} basin there is the {{ill|Kuizu Shrine|de|Kuizu-Schrein|ja|久伊豆神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|久伊豆神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuizu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久伊豆神社|1=Hisaizu-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55523154}}, and in the eastern and northern parts along the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River}} and {{ill|Edo River|en|Edo River|WD=Q1194505|lt=Edogawa River}} basins, many {{ill|Katori Jingū|lt=Katori Jingū|lt=Katori Shrine|de|Katori-Schrein|ja|香取神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 香取神社}} are found. Occasionally, merged shrines such as {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|鷲香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|香取鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Washi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} appear. Furthermore, since the term “宮” means “shrine,” many places today label their shrines as {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社|zh|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}} or {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社|zh|日鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine|1=Ame-no-Hiwashi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65270357}}, even when their old or common names remain Washi‑no‑Miya. For example, there is the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|鷲神社 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Taitō)|1=Ōtori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676284}}, in Taitō Ward, Tokyo (as inscribed on its torii plaque), the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|鷲神社 (土浦市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Tsuchiura City, Ibaraki, and the {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma City)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|日鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|1=Hiwashi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510871}}, in Minamisoma City, Fukushima.
As a {{ill|religious corporation|de|Religiöse Körperschaft|ja|宗教法人|zh|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/religious corporation|qq=}}, there are nationwide 22 Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines and 2 Washi‑no‑Miya Associations.
See also {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|de|Ōtori-Schrein|ja|大鳥神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社|zh|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}}, {{ill|Great Washi Shrine|de|Großer Washi-Schrein|ja|大鷲神社|zh|大鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Washi Shrine|1=Ōwashi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65258950}}, and {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社|zh|日鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine|1=Ame-no-Hiwashi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65270357}}.
== Washi‑no‑Miya Shrines ==
=== Fukushima Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Koriyama)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Koriyama)|ja|鷲宮神社 (郡山市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Koriyama)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Koriyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Suginoiri, Yatagawa, Tamura‑chō, Koriyama City.
=== Ibaraki Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kawachi Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Kawachi-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (河内町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kawachi Town)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Kawachi Town)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Kofabayashi, Kawachi‑chō, Inashiki District. The furigana for the corporation name is “ワシミヤジンジャ.”
=== Tochigi Prefecture === * '''{{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Toga Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Tōchigi, Toga-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (栃木市都賀町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Toga Town)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Toga Town)|1=Washinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65516640}}''' ('''Shimotsukuni Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''') – Enshrined at Ienaka, Toga Town, Tōchigi City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Hakamori Town)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Tōchigi, Hakamori-chō)|ja|鷲宮神社 (栃木市箱森町)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Hakamori Town)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Tōchigi City, Hakamori Town)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Hakamori Town, Tōchigi City.
* {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Sakura)|ja|鷲宮神社 (さくら市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sakura City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Washiyado, Sakura City.
* {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sano City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Sano)|ja|鷲宮神社 (佐野市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sano City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Sano City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Inubushi Kamimachi, Sano City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shimotsuke City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Shimotsuke)|ja|鷲宮神社 (下野市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shimotsuke City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Shimotsuke City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Hashimoto, Shimotsuke City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Ishijima)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Moka, Ishijima)|ja|鷲宮神社 (真岡市石島)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Ishijima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Ishijima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Ishijima, Moka City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Washisu)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Moka, Washisu)|ja|鷲宮神社 (真岡市鷲巣)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Washisu)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Moka City, Washisu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Washisu, Moka City.
=== Chiba Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Chiba City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Schrein (Chiba)|ja|鷲宮神社 (千葉市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Chiba City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine (Chiba City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Daiganji-chō, Chūō-ku, Chiba City.
=== Saitama Prefecture === * '''{{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|Washinomiya Shrine|WD=Q611108|lt=Washinomiya Shrine}}''' ('''Musashi‑no‑Kuni Washi‑no‑Miya Shrine''') – Enshrined at Washi‑no‑Miya, Kuki City. * {{ill|Washi‑no‑Miya Association (Kuki City)|de|Washi‑no‑Miya Gesellschaft (Kuki)|ja|鷲宮社 (久喜市)|zh|Washi‑no‑Miya Association (Kuki City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi‑no‑Miya Association == Alternate Names, Former Names, and Merged Shrines ==
=== Fukushima Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|1=Hiwashi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510871}} – Enshrined at Akechi, Onaba, Kodaka District, Minamisoma City.
=== Ibaraki Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|de|Sashinō-Schrein (Ishioka, Someya)|ja|佐志能神社 (石岡市染谷)|zh|Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|1=Sashino Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225838}} – Enshrined at Someya, Ishioka City. * {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|Katori Washi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Washi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Komagome, Koga City. * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura, Higashizakichō)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Higashizakichō, Tsuchiura City. * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Naka City)|de|Washi-Schrein (Naka)|ja|鷲神社 (那珂市)|zh|Washi Shrine (Naka City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Naka City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Washi‑no‑Miya, Hongosu, Naka City.
=== Saitama Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|de|Washi-Schrein (Saitama, Iwatsuki Sueta)|ja|鷲神社 (さいたま市岩槻区末田)|zh|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|1=Ōtori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116884577}} – Enshrined at Sueta, Iwatsuki-ku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Aoge Gochū Shrine|de|Aoge Gochū-Schrein|ja|青毛五柱神社|zh|Aoge Gochū Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoge Gochū Shrine|1=Ōgegohashira Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662567}} – Enshrined at Aoge, Kuki City. * {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Shōbu Town, Kuki City. It enshrines as its principal deities the mother {{ill|Kushinadahime|en|Kushinadahime|WD=Q505469|lt=Inada‑hime}}, also known as {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine|lt=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_ja=袋田大明神|lt_de=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_zh=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the child {{ill|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|de|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|zh|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt=Wuyi Tori no Mikoto|lt_ja=武夷鳥命|lt_de=Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt_zh=武夷鳥命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehira Tori no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ookuninushi}} (also known as {{ill|Tamatsukushi Shrine|de|Tamatsukushi-Schrein|ja|玉敷神社|zh|Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt=Kuizu Daimyōjin|lt_ja=久伊豆大明神|lt_de=Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt_zh=Kuizu Daimyōjin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsukushi Shrine|1=Tamashiki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572627}}). * {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|Washi Katori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q134886645}} – Enshrined at Uchimaki, Kasukabe City.
=== Tokyo Metropolis === * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Taitō)|1=Ōtori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676284}} – Enshrined at Senshuku, Taitō Ward.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]]
[[File:酉の市Torinoichi000.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Torinoichi|de|Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Torinoichi|ja|酉の市|zh|酉の市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinoichi|qq=}}の{{ill|Rake (tool)|en|Rake (tool)|WD=Q200822|lt=Kumade}}]] '''Washi-no-Miya Shrine''' (鷲宮神社) is the name of a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}}.
It is a branch of the Ōtori faith based on {{ill|Ōtori taisha|en|Ōtori taisha|WD=Q705151|lt=Ōtori Taisha}}, though in many cases a Washi-no-Miya Shrine serves as the head shrine of the Kantō region’s {{ill|O-Tori-sama|de|O-Tori-sama|ja|お酉様|zh|O-Tori-sama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/O-Tori-sama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市|1=Tori no Ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643942}} cult – notably the one enshrined in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}’s {{ill|Kuki, Saitama|en|Kuki, Saitama|WD=Q47535|lt=Kuki City}}. The Washi-no-Miya Shrine in Kuki City was the head shrine (総社) of the {{ill|Ōta clan|en|Ōta clan|WD=Q1391922|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato's}} {{ill|Ota Villa|de|Ota-Anwesen|ja|太田荘|zh|太田荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota Villa|1=Ota Villa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444411}}, and many branch shrines exist mainly along the {{ill|Naka River (Saitama Tokyo)|en|Naka River (Saitama Tokyo)|WD=Q2613967|lt=Nakagawa}} region. In other areas, however, Washi-no-Miya Shrines are merely branches of other Ōtori faith shrines and may have no connection to the Kuki City shrine.
In addition, along the western part of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Kantō)|en|Arakawa River (Kantō)|WD=Q625465|lt=Arakawa}} basin there is the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|en|Hikawa Shrine|WD=Q5760456|lt=Hikawa Shrine}}, along the {{ill|Moto-Arakawa|de|Moto-Arakawa|ja|元荒川|zh|元荒川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Arakawa|1=Moto-Arakawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388047}} basin there is the {{ill|Kuizu Shrine|de|Kuizu-Schrein|ja|久伊豆神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|久伊豆神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuizu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久伊豆神社|1=Hisaizu-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55523154}}, and in the eastern and northern parts along the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River}} and {{ill|Edo River|en|Edo River|WD=Q1194505|lt=Edogawa River}} basins, many {{ill|Katori Jingū|lt=Katori Jingū|lt=Katori Shrine|de|Katori-Schrein|ja|香取神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 香取神社}} are found. Occasionally, merged shrines such as {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|鷲香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|香取鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Washi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} appear. Furthermore, since the term “宮” means “shrine,” many places today label their shrines as {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社|zh|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}} or {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社|zh|日鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine|1=Ame-no-Hiwashi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65270357}}, even when their old or common names remain Washi-no-Miya. For example, there is the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|鷲神社 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Taitō)|1=Ōtori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676284}}, in Taitō Ward, Tokyo (as inscribed on its torii plaque), the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|鷲神社 (土浦市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Tsuchiura City, Ibaraki, and the {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma City)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|日鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|1=Hiwashi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510871}}, in Minamisoma City, Fukushima.
As a {{ill|religious corporation|de|Religiöse Körperschaft|ja|宗教法人|zh|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/religious corporation|qq=}}, there are nationwide 22 Washi-no-Miya Shrines and 2 Washi-no-Miya Associations.
See also {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|de|Ōtori-Schrein|ja|大鳥神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社|zh|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}}, {{ill|Great Washi Shrine|de|Großer Washi-Schrein|ja|大鷲神社|zh|大鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Washi Shrine|1=Ōwashi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65258950}}, and {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社|zh|日鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine|1=Ame-no-Hiwashi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65270357}}.

The furigana for the corporation name is “ワシミヤジンジャ.”

* {{ill|Washi-no-Miya Shrine (Sakura City)|de|Washi-no-Miya Schrein (Sakura)|ja|鷲宮神社 (さくら市)|zh|Washi-no-Miya Shrine (Sakura City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi-no-Miya Shrine (Sakura City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Washiyado, Sakura City.

District. The corporation name is “Religious Corporation Washi-no-Miya Shrine.”
=== Yamanashi Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi-no-Miya Association (Nirasaki City)|de|Washi-no-Miya Gesellschaft (Nirasaki)|ja|鷲宮社 (韮崎市)|zh|Washi-no-Miya Association (Nirasaki City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi-no-Miya Association (Nirasaki City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Anayamachō, Nirasaki City.
== Alternate Names, Former Names, and Merged Shrines ==
=== Fukushima Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|1=Hiwashi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510871}} – Enshrined at Akechi, Onaba, Kodaka District, Minamisoma City.
=== Ibaraki Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|de|Sashinō-Schrein (Ishioka, Someya)|ja|佐志能神社 (石岡市染谷)|zh|Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashinō Shrine (Ishioka City, Someya)|1=Sashino Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225838}} – Enshrined at Someya, Ishioka City. * {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|Katori Washi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Washi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Komagome, Koga City. * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura, Higashizakichō)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakichō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Higashizakichō, Tsuchiura City. * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Naka City)|de|Washi-Schrein (Naka)|ja|鷲神社 (那珂市)|zh|Washi Shrine (Naka City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Naka City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Washi-no-Miya, Hongosu, Naka City.
=== Saitama Prefecture (Alternate/Former/Merged) === * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|de|Washi-Schrein (Saitama, Iwatsuki Sueta)|ja|鷲神社 (さいたま市岩槻区末田)|zh|Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Saitama City, Iwatsuki-ku, Sueta)|1=Ōtori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116884577}} – Enshrined at Sueta, Iwatsuki-ku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Aoge Gochū Shrine|de|Aoge Gochū-Schrein|ja|青毛五柱神社|zh|Aoge Gochū Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoge Gochū Shrine|1=Ōgegohashira Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662567}} – Enshrined at Aoge, Kuki City. * {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Shōbu Town, Kuki City. It enshrines as its principal deities the mother {{ill|Kushinadahime|en|Kushinadahime|WD=Q505469|lt=Inada-hime}}, also known as {{ill|Shōbu Shrine|de|Shōbu-Schrein|ja|菖蒲神社|zh|Shōbu Shrine|lt=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_ja=袋田大明神|lt_de=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|lt_zh=Fukuroda Daimyōjin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the child {{ill|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|de|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|zh|Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt=Wuyi Tori no Mikoto|lt_ja=武夷鳥命|lt_de=Takehira Tori no Mikoto|lt_zh=武夷鳥命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehira Tori no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ookuninushi}} (also known as {{ill|Tamatsukushi Shrine|de|Tamatsukushi-Schrein|ja|玉敷神社|zh|Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt=Kuizu Daimyōjin|lt_ja=久伊豆大明神|lt_de=Tamatsukushi Shrine|lt_zh=Kuizu Daimyōjin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsukushi Shrine|1=Tamashiki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572627}}). * {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|Washi Katori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q134886645}} – Enshrined at Uchimaki, Kasukabe City.
=== Tokyo Metropolis === * {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Taitō)|1=Ōtori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676284}} – Enshrined at Senshuku, Taitō Ward.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鷲宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)]]
[[File:酉の市Torinoichi000.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Torinoichi|de|Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Tori-no-Ichi|lt=Torinoichi|ja|酉の市|zh|酉の市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinoichi|qq=}}の{{ill|Rake (tool)|en|Rake (tool)|WD=Q200822|lt=Kumade|1=Rake (tool)}}]] '''Washi-no-Miya Shrine''' (鷲宮神社) is the name of a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}.
It is a branch of the Ōtori faith based on {{ill|Ōtori taisha|en|Ōtori taisha|WD=Q705151|lt=Ōtori Taisha|1=Ōtori taisha}}, though in many cases a Washi-no-Miya Shrine serves as the head shrine of the Kantō region’s {{ill|O-Tori-sama|de|O-Tori-sama|ja|お酉様|zh|O-Tori-sama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/O-Tori-sama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643942}} cult – notably the one enshrined in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}’s {{ill|Kuki, Saitama|en|Kuki, Saitama|WD=Q47535|lt=Kuki City|1=Kuki, Saitama}}. The Washi-no-Miya Shrine in Kuki City was the head shrine (総社) of the {{ill|Ōta clan|en|Ōta clan|WD=Q1391922|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato's|1=Ōta clan}} {{ill|Ota Villa|de|Ota-Anwesen|ja|太田荘|zh|太田荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota Villa|1=Q11444411|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444411}}, and many branch shrines exist mainly along the {{ill|Naka River (Saitama Tokyo)|en|Naka River (Saitama Tokyo)|WD=Q2613967|lt=Nakagawa|1=Naka River (Saitama Tokyo)}} region. In other areas, however, Washi-no-Miya Shrines are merely branches of other Ōtori faith shrines and may have no connection to the Kuki City shrine.
In addition, along the western part of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Kantō)|en|Arakawa River (Kantō)|WD=Q625465|lt=Arakawa|1=Arakawa River (Kantō)}} basin there is the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|en|Hikawa Shrine|WD=Q5760456|lt=Hikawa Shrine|1=Hikawa Shrine}}, along the {{ill|Moto-Arakawa|de|Moto-Arakawa|ja|元荒川|zh|元荒川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Arakawa|1=Moto-Arakawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388047}} basin there is the {{ill|Kuizu Shrine|de|Kuizu-Schrein|ja|久伊豆神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|久伊豆神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuizu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久伊豆神社|1=Hisaizu-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55523154}}, and in the eastern and northern parts along the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River|1=Tone River}} and {{ill|Edo River|en|Edo River|WD=Q1194505|lt=Edogawa River|1=Edo River}} basins, many {{ill|Katori Jingū|lt=Katori Jingū|lt=Katori Shrine|de|Katori-Schrein|ja|香取神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 香取神社}} are found. Occasionally, merged shrines such as {{ill|Washi Katori Shrine|de|Washi-Katori-Schrein|ja|鷲香取神社|zh|鷲香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Katori Washi Shrine|de|Katori-Washi-Schrein|ja|香取鷲神社|zh|香取鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Washi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} appear. Furthermore, since the term “宮” means “shrine,” many places today label their shrines as {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社|zh|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}} or {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社|zh|日鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine|1=Ame-no-Hiwashi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65270357}}, even when their old or common names remain Washi-no-Miya. For example, there is the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Taitō)|de|Washi-Schrein (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|zh|鷲神社 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Taitō)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676284}}, in Taitō Ward, Tokyo (as inscribed on its torii plaque), the {{ill|Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|de|Washi-Schrein (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja|鷲神社 (土浦市東崎町)|zh|鷲神社 (土浦市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine (Tsuchiura City, Higashizakicho)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Tsuchiura City, Ibaraki, and the {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein (Minamisoma City)|ja|日鷲神社 (南相馬市)|zh|日鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine (Minamisoma City)|1=Hiwashi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510871}}, in Minamisoma City, Fukushima.
As a {{ill|religious corporation|de|Religiöse Körperschaft|ja|宗教法人|zh|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/religious corporation|qq=}}, there are nationwide 22 Washi-no-Miya Shrines and 2 Washi-no-Miya Associations.
See also {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|de|Ōtori-Schrein|ja|大鳥神社 (曖昧さ回避)|zh|大鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Washi Shrine|de|Washi-Schrein|ja|鷲神社|zh|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}}, {{ill|Great Washi Shrine|de|Großer Washi-Schrein|ja|大鷲神社|zh|大鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Washi Shrine|1=Ōwashi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65258950}}, and {{ill|Nichiwashi Shrine|de|Nichiwashi-Schrein|ja|日鷲神社|zh|日鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiwashi Shrine|1=Ame-no-Hiwashi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65270357}}.
== Washi-no-Miya Shrines ==
=== Fukushima Prefecture === * {{ill|Washi-no-Miya Shrine (Koriyama)|de|Washi-no-Miya Schrein (Koriyama)|ja|鷲宮神社 (郡山市)|zh|Washi-no-Miya Shrine (Koriyama)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washi-no-Miya Shrine (Koriyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined at Suginoiri, Yatagawa, Tamura-chō, Koriyama City.
